title: ICT Applications Support Analyst (Grade V)
description:
Job Title: ICT Applications Support Analyst (Grade V)
Reference No: 04.024 (2026)
Department / Directorate: ICT Department, ICT Directorate
Reports to: ICT Applications Portfolio Manager or Deputy

Tallaght University Hospital (TUH) is a model 4, voluntary, academic teaching hospital partnered with Trinity College and situated in south west Dublin. With a staff of over 4,000 people from 63 different countries, it provides both acute and tertiary care to an increasing population of circa 800,000 people and is a provider of local, regional and national specialties. It is also a National Urology Centre, the second largest provider of dialysis services in the country, Pelvic and Acetabulum National Centre and a designated Trauma Unit.

The Hospital is part of the newly established HSE Dublin & Midlands Region, which serves a population of over 1.2 million across seven counties, and operates under the governance of an independent Board of Directors who give their time on a voluntary basis. TUH is also supported by the Adelaide Health Foundation, the Meath Foundation and Tallaght University Hospital Foundation.

TUH recognises the role it plays in our community both as an employer and provider of healthcare and is committed to building a sustainable future for our region both now and into the future with a focus on patient outcomes.

The Hospital is ambitious, recognising that healthcare delivery is undergoing seismic change and will be different now and into the future, largely due to digital and technological advances along with research continuing to discover and test new treatment options. TUH wants to be a leader in determining that future. It is a “Hospital without Walls” always looking to optimise care both within and beyond in line with the Sláintecare vision of providing the right care, in the right place, at the right time whilst empowering our patients and staff.

TUH Vision and Values
The vision of the Hospital is “People Caring for People to Live Better Lives” through excellent health outcomes supported by evidenced based practice, positive patient and staff experience in an empowering & caring environment. A culture of innovation and quality improvement in everything we do.

Our TUH CARE values – for patients, their families, our community and staff are:
  • Collaborate – together and with our academic and care partners
  • Achieve – our goals, positive outcomes and wellbeing
  • Respect – for patients, each other and our environment
  • Equity – for patients and staff

At TUH we view our staff as our most valuable asset and every member of the Team is valued equally. We recognise that a skilled, satisfied and motivated workforce is a prerequisite to high quality care.

A full overview of TUH is available on www.tuh.ie/about us

Qualifications & Experience required

Must have:
  • Have obtained at least Grade D3 in five subjects in the Leaving Certificate Examination (higher, ordinary, applied or vocational programmes) or equivalent
Or
  • Have passed an examination at the appropriate level within the QQI qualifications framework which can be assessed as being of a comparable to Leaving Certificate standard or equivalent or higher i.e. Fetac Level 5
Or
  • Have satisfactory relevant experience which encompasses demonstrable equivalent skills
And
  • Undertaken a relevant 3rd level ICT qualification or relevant course of study.
  • Have at least 2 years’ experience in the software applications support, issue troubleshooting, application administration and configuration.
  • Have experience working with suppliers on troubleshooting issues, reviewing and testing application changes and fixes.

Desirable:
  • ITIL qualification and/or experience.
  • Knowledge of Change Management and/or related experience in software application changes.
  • Knowledge on ICT procedures and regulations.
  • Experience and knowledge on hospital/healthcare systems and familiarity with medical terminology.

Behavioural Requirements:
  • Experienced in successfully liaising and working collaboratively with users and between groups, in addition to excellent interpersonal and communications skills (oral and written).
  • Excellent problem solving, planning, organisation and analytical skills with the ability to make sense of complex and logical problems quickly.
  • Ability to plan & prioritise work, meet deadlines while responding flexibly to rapidly changing priorities.
  • Ability to work unsupervised and to use initiative when necessary to deal with conflicting priorities calmly and effectively.
  • Ability to identify and recommend process and/or application improvements.
  • Ability to frame options and present recommendations.
  • Experience of working in a demanding environment and ability to negotiate and manage through conflict and to deal with ambiguity.

Purpose of the Role:
The ICT Applications Support Analyst is responsible for delivering day-to-day support and maintenance for assigned ICT application systems. This role focuses on providing application administration and ensuring the stability and reliability of critical information systems and processes. The analyst will collaborate with suppliers and hospital stakeholders to deliver high-quality application support services. This is an application support role and does not involve software development or coding activities. The role reports to the ICT Application Portfolio Manager, Deputy, or Team Lead and operates as part of the ICT Applications Portfolio Management Team.

title: IT Service Desk Analyst
description:
Company Description


At Frasers Group we’re rethinking retail. Through digital innovation and unique store experiences, we’re serving our consumers with the world’s best sports, premium and luxury brands globally. As a leader in the industry, we’re elevating the retail experience for our consumers through our collection of established brands, including Sports Direct, FLANNELS, USC, Frasers, and GAME.

Why join us?

Our vision- we are building the worlds most admired and compelling brand ecosystem

Our purpose – we are elevating the lives of the many with access to the world’s best brands and experiences

At Frasers Group, we fear less and do more. Our people are forward thinkers who are driven to operate outside of their comfort zone to change the future of retail, embracing challenges along the way. The potential to elevate your career is massive, the experience unrivalled. To be able to make the most of it you need to live and breathe our principles:

  • Think without limits - Think fast, think fearlessly, and take the team with you
  • Own it and back yourself - Own the basics, own your role and own the results
  • Be relevant - Relevant to our people, our partners and the planet

Are you ready to join the Fearless?


Job Description


We are currently looking for an IT Service Desk Analyst to join our team in the Dublin office.

As part of the role you will be owning IT support in our Dublin Head office as your base work location, along with our providing support for our wider business. Providing face to face support, using your problem solving ability and providing a timely resolution to IT incidents via our ITSM tool. You will also be responsible for assetizing, configuring and preparing hardware and software instillations for our end users efficiently. Along with creating and updating any technical documentation and sharing with the team. In line with this you will also deliver exceptional end user service via our telephony and ticketing systems.

This role is based in our Dublin office, Monday to Friday, working shifts where required.


Qualifications


Required:

  • Minimum of 1 year experience in an IT Support role
  • Can work proactively, independently and collaborate well with the wider teams
  • Able to effectively troubleshoot issues and use initiative during problem solving
  • A team player who can communicate effectively with colleagues at all levels
  • Be positive, friendly and approachable
  • Experience with face-to-face IT support
  • Customer focused and Resolution driven.

The below skills are desirable as full training will be provided in our Training Academy:

  • Hardware configuration and installation
  • SQL Query language
  • Active Directory, Group Policy, DNS, DHCP etc
  • Cisco networking systems
  • Windows desktop and server operating systems

Additional Information


Additional Information

Along with your benefits package we also offer a wide range of perks for our colleagues:

Frasers Champion- Our employees are at the heart of our business and we ensure individuals are recognised every single month for their hard work. Frasers Champion is a peer nominated scheme where 8 winners will receive double their pay for a month where they have lived the Frasers Group values.

Retail Reconnect – In order to build the planets most admired and compelling brand ecosystem, all employees must understand our business, product and customers. Each financial year, head office employees will gain insights by spending one to two days in one of our stores or the warehouse. The goal is to learn how the work you do impacts our teams on the frontline, and to bring ideas back to the office which will improve how we work.

Fearless 1200 – Fearless 1200 is our way of recognising our growth. It’s bold, ambitious, and designed to reward colleagues across the business for living our values and delivering impact. Fearless 1200 links how we perform to how we're rewarded – check out the video link to find out more - https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=O0qmvJofMew

Employee Welfare

Frasers Fit – Our Everlast Gyms Team are on a mission to make our workforce the best, and fittest on the planet! We run free gym classes for employees as well as discounted memberships to our clubs. Frasers Fit is our wellbeing programme which aims to support and improve colleagues Physical, Financial & Mental wellbeing. The app is accessible for every employee and includes training, nutrition and lifestyle advice- all completely free.

Retail Trust – We know that its not just about physical health, mental wellness is equally important which is why all of our employees get free access and support from the Retail Trust charity. This includes a 24 hour wellbeing helpline, wellness hub, counselling and financial/legal support.

What’s next?

Our Recruitment Team will be reviewing applications and all candidates will receive a response, whether you are successful or unsuccessful. Shortlisted applicants may be asked to confirm a few key details before being booked in for a first stage interview with the Recruiter - this will be behaviourally focussed and centred around how you align with our Culture and Values. If successful we anticipate a further interview stage with the Hiring Manager/wider team.

title: Technical Business Systems Analyst (BSA)
description:
If you need support in completing the application or if you require a different format of this document, please get in touch with us at UKI.Recruitment@tcs.com with the subject line: “Application Support Request”.

Job Type: Permanent
Location: Letterkenny GDC, Co. Donegal (Hybrid – minimum 3 days onsite per week)

Do you have strong financial services business knowledge?
Do you want to deepen your financial services expertise while working closely with data and technology teams?
Make your mark at TCS as a Technical Business Systems Analyst (BSA)!

Join a global tech leader right here in Donegal. With over 850 employees and a state-of-the-art global delivery centre, we’re proud to offer world-class career opportunities without having to leave the Northwest. Recognised as Workplace of the Year by the Letterkenny Chamber, we foster a culture of continuous learning, innovation, and respect. Our people are at the heart of everything we do – collaborating across teams, geographies, and disciplines to drive real change for clients around the world. Be part of something global, grounded in Donegal.

TCS is a purpose-led transformation company, built on belief. We do not just help businesses to transform through technology. We support them in making a meaningful difference to the people and communities they serve - our clients include some of the biggest brands in the UK and worldwide. For you, it means more to make an impact that matters, through challenging projects which demand ambitious innovation and thought leadership.
  • Join a leading global IT services organisation.
  • Be part of an exciting team where you will be challenged every day.
  • Gain exposure to innovative technology.

The Role
We are seeking an experienced Technical Business Systems Analyst to support a global financial services client environment. This role sits at the intersection of business, data, and technology, and plays a key role in enabling data‑driven decision making, supporting critical platforms, and delivering change across business and technology teams.
The role is heavily focused on BAU data provisioning with end‑to‑end ownership of data onboarding, issue investigation and resolution, and continuous improvement, operating within an Agile delivery model.
The successful candidate will be hands‑on, proactive, and accountable, with strong financial services / capital markets domain knowledge, solid SQL skills, and the ability to decompose complex business logic, understand existing technical implementations, and work closely with business stakeholders and engineering teams to ensure the reliable, high‑quality delivery of data products.
Key Responsibilities:
  • Own end‑to‑end delivery and support of financial services data products, from requirement intake through production support, ensuring stability, accuracy, and timeliness.
  • Act as the primary Technical BSA for BAU and production data issues, taking accountability for root cause analysis, remediation, and prevention of recurring issues.
  • Partner with business stakeholders to translate financial domain business needs into clear, actionable functional and technical specifications, including data mappings, transformations, and validation rules.
  • Work closely with data engineering and technology teams to analyze existing data logic, review SQL and code, and validate that implementations align with business intent.
  • Proactively monitor and assess data quality, identify issues early, and drive resolution through to completion rather than simple escalation.
  • Support Agile delivery by contributing to backlog refinement, sprint planning, story breakdown, acceptance criteria, and release readiness.
  • Collaborate with Front Office, Risk, Operations, and downstream consumers to clarify requirements, resolve data questions, and ensure shared understanding of outputs and impacts.
  • Ensure data solutions meet production‑grade standards, including stability, accuracy, traceability, auditability, and operational resilience.
  • Continuously improve BAU processes, controls, and documentation, reducing operational risk and improving transparency and efficiency.
  • Proactively identify opportunities for process optimisation, automation, and improved data controls, incorporating best practices across financial services data delivery.

Your Profile
Essential skills/knowledge/experience:
  • Bachelor’s degree in computer science, Information Systems, Data Analytics, Business Information Systems, or a related discipline.
  • 5+ years proven experience in a Technical Business Analyst, Business Systems Analyst, Data Analyst, or similar role within financial services / capital markets.
  • Hands‑on experience supporting investment, trading, risk, operations, or regulatory reporting data platforms in a production environment.
  • Experience working with industry‑standard investment or portfolio management platforms.
  • Strong understanding of financial services data domains, with the ability to engage confidently with Front Office, Risk, Operations, and Technology stakeholders without heavy translation layers.
  • Demonstrated experience acting as a primary BA/BSA for BAU and production‑critical issues, with clear ownership from investigation through resolution.
  • Strong analytical and problem‑solving skills, particularly in the context of complex data, business logic, and operational issues.
  • Excellent communication skills, with the ability to explain complex data concepts clearly to both technical

title: Data Operations Analyst - Outbound Reporting
description:
Company Overview:

Citco Fund Services companies is the world's pre-eminent hedge fund administrator with over 40 years’ experience in the provision of administration and extended value-add services to hedge fund clients - many of whom are leading names in the industry. Operating out of 19 strategic centers globally, our global presence reflects our philosophy to provide support wherever our clients are located. This approach gives us an opportunity to utilize this global reach to ensure specific client needs are met on a timely basis.

Citco Fund Services offer a full range of fund administration services and complete front-to-back offering for single manager funds, combining portfolio capture and real-time position monitoring technology, using the Æxeo Technology system, with middle and back office operations support.

A career at Citco isn’t just a job – it’s an opportunity to excel in an environment that genuinely supports your personal and professional development.

Department Overview:

Data operations is responsible for all aspects of portfolio data exchange and support across trade processing, pricing and position data. The group has an operational and a strategic mandate to fulfill. This is accomplished through a location strategy that combines presence in strategic locations close to our client needs and the utilization of standardization, centralization and scale in our operational Centre of Excellence (CoE) locations.

Operational:

  • Based on requests from internal Operations and Client Integration teams, manage requests for standard extracts (Spos 4x, Standard Trade, Taxlot)
  • Configuration & maintenance of electronic interfaces that exchange outbound data from Citco applications.
  • Provide level one support, troubleshooting errors for standard extracts

Strategic:

  • Provide leadership in establishing standards & benchmark in the area of portfolio data interfaces - for Citco and the industry as a result.
  • Collaborate with Citco Technology to analyze, develop & enhance internal tools that support increased levels of automation and intuitive self-service for operations.

Position Overview

The successful candidate will be part of the Data Operations Outbound Reporting team department whose primary responsibility will be to ensure the effective management of all aspects for outbound extracts to Clients and Vendors.

He/she will be part of the Data Operations Outbound Reporting Team whose responsibility will be to ensure the effective management of all aspects for currently supported reconciliations, including data extracts, instructs, swift and payments. The reporting team owns the management of all aspects of client outbound reporting needs and ongoing support of interfaces including client on-boarding and client relationship management. As a group with representation in Asia Pacific, Europe and North America, the team provides seamless support to all CFS offices on a global basis.

The Reporting Analyst will have the ability to follow processes and have a fundamental understanding of complex financial instruments. The successful candidate will be responsible for creating, monitoring and maintaining standard extracts and provide support to business teams or clients as queries arrive .He/she will demonstrate ability to multi task on multiple items based on the functional priorities and objectives. The candidate will have strong communication skills, as well as technical ability to understanding the configurations and file flows involved in the role.

Main Job Functions/Responsibilities

  • Responsible for Standard extracts requests and support, including investigation and management of file generation or data issues
  • Communicate effectively with both internal and external parties to discuss reporting issues. This includes clients, vendors and counterparties addition to internal CFS teams.
  • Takes a proactive approach to solving issues, displays initiative and good example by contributing ideas for increased efficiencies and/or automation of the various support procedures.
  • Work with Client Integration on the creation of new standard Spos/STRD/Taxlot extracts
  • Engage with Data management to ensure correct delivery of standard extracts
  • Configure and maintain reporting interfaces as per internal and external business instructions, including investigation and address post-implementation discrepancies as required.
  • Safely retire & decommission legacy reports that are no longer required
  • Assist Management in design and implementation of workflow processes and improvements.
  • Identify opportunities for efficiencies, risk mitigation, standardization and automation and work with managers and team to translate into actionable projects.
  • Display strong solving skills. Candidates are required to research problems reported by operations teams to determine if an actual problem exists or if additional training is required. When actual problem exists, they need to find workaround in a timely fashion
  • Ability to explain technical concepts to users in terms that can be easily understood.

Education & Experiences

  • Degree qualified in an IT/Business related discipline with a keen interest in financial services
  • Ideally, 1 years’ experience in an operations processing or related support environment
  • Ability to understand source systems, data dependencies, interfaces, data integration and transformation
  • General knowledge of databases or experience using SQL Server (T

title: IT Systems Administrator - North County Dublin
description:
Location: North County Dublin
Job Type: Contract 6 Months - (with option to extend for a further 6 Months)
Work Type: Hybrid - onsite presence of 3 days per week for the duration of the contract
Role Type: Technical / IT Infrastructure & Security

Overview of Role
The IT Operations Systems Administrator is responsible for the design, build, support, and maintenance of complex Microsoft-based infrastructure solutions, with a strong emphasis on Cyber Security. Acting as the primary interface between Cyber Security project teams and IT Operations, the role focuses on delivering secure infrastructure initiatives, enhancing organisational security posture, and ensuring alignment with operational and architectural standards.

Role & Responsibilities:

  • Function as technical lead for Cyber Security infrastructure initiatives in a 24x7 operational environment.
  • Design, build, and implement secure, scalable, and resilient Microsoft-based infrastructure solutions.
  • Manage vulnerability remediation and security patching processes.
  • Produce high-quality documentation to support audits and compliance requirements.
  • Perform system administration tasks to maintain system health and stability.
  • Recommend and implement improvements to operational processes and procedures.
  • Implement high availability and disaster recovery solutions for critical systems.
  • Work closely with application teams to ensure integration's are secure, efficient, and perform reliably
  • Provide escalation support for complex technical issues.
  • Mentor junior support staff and contribute to team development.
  • Monitor infrastructure and security performance to meet SLA requirements.
  • Develop and test fail-over and resilience strategies for critical services.


Skills, Qualifications & Experience

  • Strong expertise in Microsoft technologies, including Windows Server (2019/2022/2025) and Active Directory.
  • In-depth knowledge of Group Policy, networking concepts, and security best practices.
  • Experience with backup technologies (NetBackup desirable).
  • Familiarity with Windows 11 client environments.
  • Understanding of VMware and storage technologies (desirable).
  • Exposure to Linux environments (RedHat/OEL desirable).
  • Strong analytical, troubleshooting, and critical thinking skills.
  • Experience working within ITIL or structured process environments.
  • Automation knowledge (Python, Ansible, REST APIs desirable).
  • Effective communication, documentation, and organisational skills.


Experience:

  • 5+ year's experience in Microsoft infrastructure within a large enterprise environment.
  • Proven experience delivering secure infrastructure solutions and supporting Cyber Security initiatives.
  • Experience working in high-availability, mission-critical environments.


Education & Certifications:

  • Third-level qualification in IT or equivalent experience.
  • Relevant certifications such as MCSA, MCP, CCNA, CompTIA Security+, or Microsoft Certified Information Security Administrator Associate (Azure/M365 focus).


Visa:

  • Irish/EU Citizen or Stamp 4 Visa holder.


About Us
As Ireland's largest IT Solutions Provider with a growing global footprint, ergo offers deep expertise across managed IT & hardware services, cloud, applications & software services, IT resourcing, and modern work solutions. Our recent technology awards reflect our commitment to delivering industry-leading IT solutions, highlighting our team's dedication and expertise.

title: Business Process Engineer
description:
In today’s business climate, a majority of a company’s operating knowledge is in people’s heads, paper, or cloud/network drives, and the speed at which they are moving prohibits them from documenting this critical intellectual property. As a result, companies can be exposed to disruption in the servicing of their clients and the operation of their business. Our goal is simple: to help companies take control of their Operational IP — to drive quality and consistency so companies and their customers can thrive.The Confidence platform enables companies to define and document business processes and derive real‑time data on the execution of these processes to measure productivity and efficiency. The system incorporates AI to automate parts of the process and verify that work was completed correctly. Learn more at: https://confidence.com/We are a Silicon Valley–based technology company re‑imagining the way work gets done. The founding team is a product development powerhouse, led by our Founding CEO, a seasoned entrepreneur with exits to Amazon and the author of Winning @ Product Development.The RoleConfidence is seeking a Business Process Engineer who will work directly with large enterprise clients to capture and document institutional knowledge using the Confidence platform. This is a client‑facing role combining process engineering and optimization with software implementation and adoption. You will join our Process Engineering team within Delivery and report to the Director of Delivery.What you’ll doWork with clients to understand processes performed by stakeholders and translate them into efficient, quality‑controlled workflows in the Confidence platform.Become an expert user of the Confidence platform and provide live product demonstrations.Recommend product enhancements based on client feedback and usage.Identify milestones, dependencies and exception use cases; advise where specific Confidence features add value.Collect and organize information from multiple sources to recommend improvements and standardization.Train client stakeholders on effective use of the platform.Analyze client data and build business cases demonstrating the platform’s value.RequirementsBachelor’s degree2+ years’ professional experience in process improvement, industrial engineering, business process engineering, or a related field.Structured and inquisitive approach with a focus on user‑friendly solutions.Comfortable working in a fast‑paced, results‑driven startup environment.Strong organization, communication and relationship‑building skills with internal teams and external clients.Strong analytical and critical thinking; able to interpret and summarize data clearly; proficient in Excel.C1–C2 level English (written and spoken).Occasional travel (<10%).Right to work in IrelandBonusDegree in Industrial Engineering or related discipline.Proven track record leading projects with measurable outcomes.Experience with SQL, Python, or similar analytical/programming languages.Experience developing and presenting business cases to senior stakeholders.BenefitsFully remote (Ireland)Discretionary Time OffCompetitive Compensation PackagePrivate health insurance or allowance (where applicable).Pension contribution / employer PRSA or equivalent.SalaryThe base salary ranges for this role in Ireland are:Level 1: €45,000 – €55,000Level 2: €55,000 – €70,000Level 3: €70,000 – €85,000Grade level and salary are determined through interviews and a review of education, experience, skills and geographic location. The base salary range does not include any equity or additional benefits.Please note that this position is open only to individuals who have the legal right to work in Ireland and are currently residing in Ireland.

Turn your ambition into action— and shape your future with us.


Job Description:


Please note that for these graduate opportunities we are happy for you to simply apply with a CV and the option to include a cover letter.


At Mott MacDonald, we believe engineering is more than solving problems — it’s about shaping a better world. As a Graduate Overhead Engineer, you’ll be part of a collaborative, inclusive, and forward-thinking team that designs and delivers sustainable solutions across sectors including energy, aviation, buildings, ports, rail & metros, and water.


Candidate Specification:


We are looking for committed and motivated graduates with a genuine passion and a desire to make a difference in the world. If this describes you, apply today to launch your career at Mott MacDonald.


To be eligible for this opportunity, you will have less than 12 months relevant experience and have a degree or are expected to achieve one of the following degree disciplines:


  • Electrical Engineering

  • Civil Engineering


In your application you should be able to demonstrate your interest in the role and working at Mott MacDonald through relevant experience, such as studying relevant modules, or previous work experience.


We are looking for graduates with the following characteristics:


  • Methodical approach to problem solving.

  • Ability to use your initiative to undertake tasks efficiently and independently.

  • Excellent verbal communication skills, which allow you to confidently liaise with clients and team members.

  • Excellent written communication and attention to detail and be able to demonstrate accurate technical drawings and good report writing.

  • As we want the best people for the role, we support flexible working. Please ask us at interview stage about any flexibility you may need.


As we want the best people for the role, we support flexible working. Please ask us at interview stage about any flexibility you may need.


Mott MacDonald Ltd. are not currently offering sponsorship to candidates under the Skilled Worker visa route in the UK. This decision is as a consequence of the changes made to the Skilled Worker route by the UK Government in April 2024.


Our ethos is to develop, train and retain our graduates and therefore we are looking for a long-term relationship with our graduates. For this reason, we cannot accept applications from candidates who would be working under the Graduate visa route or other time-limited visas, (irrespective of visa duration), due to their temporary nature and the requirement for sponsorship under the Skilled Worker route in future.


We continue to welcome applications from candidates who are eligible for alternative immigration routes in the UK, that do not require sponsorship as a Skilled Worker now or in future.


About your development


A graduate position should be more than just a job. We know this and so do you. That’s why with our graduate roles we aim to give you the experience and technical knowledge you need to progress your career. From the moment you join us, you’ll receive the training you need. You will be assigned a mentor, who will guide you to meet the objectives of your professional training needs. You will join our three-year early careers soft skills development programme – Accelerating your Future, which is designed to give you the skills and tools necessary for a long-lasting successful career here at Mott MacDonald.


You will have the opportunity to make a difference; learn more about Our Purpose and the difference we can make!


You’re probably wondering what else is on offer. Join us, and you’ll get:



  • Biannual salary reviews: we believe that hard work should be rewarded and recognised. Therefore, for the first three years of your career with us, you’ll have biannual salary reviews.
  • A competitive salary: in addition to biannual reviews, we will ensure that you’re given a salary that matches the current industry standard.
  • Contributory pension up to 7% of your salary: we have the best people on our team, and we like to look out for them. With our support, you’ll have all the advice and options you need to be able to invest in your future.
  • A flexible benefits scheme: our company is made up of a range of different people and we understand that different people want different things. That’s why wi

title: Project Officer - Health Information and Standards / Oifigeach Tionscadail- Faisnéis agus Caighdeáin Sláinte

HIQA is an independent authority established to drive high-quality and safe care for people using our health and social care services in Ireland. Our role is to develop standards, inspect and review health and social care services and support informed decisions on how services are delivered. If you are focused, motivated and seeking a change in your career, then have a look at our current vacancies.

The Health Information and Quality Authority is an equal opportunities employer.


Project Officer - Health Information and Standards

Location: Dublin, Cork or Galway
Grade: Engineer Grade III
Tenure: 2 x Permanent Role, 1 x Fixed Term Role until 28th July 2027

HIQA has a role to promote safety and quality in the provision of health and social care services for the benefit of the health and welfare of the public. There are five different functions within the Health Information and Standards Directorate which are outlined below. These roles will work within the National Care Experience Programme (NCEP). NCEP carry out national service-user experience surveys and engagements across a range of Health services, in conjunction with the Department of Health, the Mental Health Commission and the HSE. Home - National Care Experience Programme. There are a number of roles available within the NCEP, they will either work in the operational or the data and digital stream. The core purpose is to support the team to meet its operational or data management commitments. It includes project management, communication, administration, supporting the team to meet best practice in relation to data management requirements, and all roles involve extensive stakeholder engagement. Project Officers play a key role in supporting the operational or data management stream within the NCEP function, ensuring tasks are achieved in a timely and professional manner.

Essential:

  • Degree qualification (minimum of Level 8 on the National Framework of Qualifications or equivalent) in a professional health and social care qualification, health related science, public health, business management, operations, or an area deemed relevant to the role by the Authority

and

  • A minimum of 12 months’ post-graduate experience in the following areas (note: work conducted as part-fulfilment of an academic award at degree or masters level should not be included here):
    • Practical experience in project management and operational support
    • Experience with data management, digital design or working with communication teams
    • Experience in writing, reviewing, editing and proofing documents and reports for a variety of audiences.

Benefits of working at HIQA:

  • Permanent position
  • 25 days annual leave
  • Pension Scheme
  • Learning and Development Opportunities
  • Well-being Programme
  • Social Club
  • Employee Assistance Programme
  • Academic Education Support Scheme.


The completed application form must be submitted by 5:00pm Monday 18th May 2026. Only web applications fully completed will be accepted. CVs will not be accepted. The Health Information and Quality Authority is an equal opportunities employer.

Oifigeach Tionscadail- Faisnéis agus Caighdeáin Sláinte

Láthair: Baile Átha Cliath, Corcaigh nó Gaillimh
Grád: Innealtóir Grád III
Tionacht: 2 x Ról Buan, 1 x Ról Téarma Sheasta go dtí an 28 Iúil 2027

Tá ról ag HIQA maidir le sábháilteacht agus cáilíocht a chur chun cinn i soláthar seirbhísí sláinte agus seirbhísí cúraim shóisialta ar mhaithe le sláinte agus leas an phobail. Tá cúig fheidhm dhifriúla leis an Stiúrthóireacht um Fhaisnéis agus Caighdeáin Sláinte agus leagtar amach thíos iad. Beidh na róil sin ag feidhmiú laistigh den Chlár Náisiúnta um Eispéiris Chúraim (NCEP). Déanann NCEP suirbhéanna agus rannpháirt náisiúnta maidir le heispéiris úsáideoirí seirbhísí ar fud réimse seirbhísí Sláinte, i gcomhar leis an Roinn Sláinte, an Coimisiún Meabhair-Shláinte agus FSS. Baile - An Clár Náisiúnta um Eispéiris Chúraim. Tá roinnt ról ar fáil in NCEP, agus oibreoidh siad sa sruth digiteach oibríochtúil nó sonraí. An príomhchuspóir atá leis is ea tacú leis an bhfoireann a cuid gealltanas oibríochtúla nó bainistíochta sonraí a chomhlíonadh. Áirítear leis bainistíocht tionscadail, cumarsáid, riarachán, tacú leis an bhfoireann an dea-chleachtas a shásamh i dtaca le riachtanais bainistíochta sonraí, agus baineann rannpháirteachas forleathan le páirtithe leasmhara le gach ról. Bíonn ról lárnach ag Oifigigh Tionscadail i dtacú leis an sruth oibríochtúil nó bainistíochta sonraí san fheidhm NCEP, trína chinntiú go mbaintear tascanna amach ar bhealach tráthúil agus proifisiúnta.

Riachtanach:

  • Cáilíocht chéime (Leibhéal 8 ar an gCreat Náisiúnta Cáilíochtaí nó a chomhionann) i gcáilíocht ghairmiúil sláinte agus c&uacu

title: Graduate Engineer - Drogheda
description:
About the Role:


EPS Group is seeking a motivated Graduate Engineer to join the project team delivering the Drogheda Wastewater Treatment Plant Upgrade. This is an excellent opportunity for a recently qualified engineer to gain hands-on experience on a large-scale wastewater infrastructure project, working alongside experienced engineers, project managers and operational teams.

The successful candidate will support the engineering, construction, commissioning and handover phases of the project, with exposure to wastewater process engineering, mechanical and electrical installations, health & safety and project delivery within a regulated environment.


Key Responsibilities:


  • Support senior engineers and project managers in the delivery of the Drogheda WWTP upgrade project
  • Assist with engineering design reviews, technical documentation and drawings
  • Participate in site activities including inspections, surveys and construction support
  • Coordinate with subcontractors, suppliers and internal EPS teams
  • Assist with method statements, risk assessments and compliance documentation
  • Support monitoring of project progress, schedules and quality standards
  • Assist with commissioning, testing and handover processes
  • Ensure compliance with health, safety, environmental and quality standards
  • Maintain accurate project records and reports


Key Competencies & Skills:


Essential:

  • Bachelor's degree in Civil Engineering, Mechanical Engineering, Electrical Engineering, Environmental Engineering or a related discipline
  • Strong interest in wastewater treatment, utilities or infrastructure projects
  • Good communication and organisational skills
  • Ability to work effectively as part of a team and on own initiative
  • Full driving licence (or working towards)


Desirable:

  • Previous placement, internship or graduate experience in engineering or construction
  • Knowledge of wastewater treatment processes or water infrastructure
  • Familiarity with AutoCAD, MS Project or similar engineering tools
  • Understanding of Irish health & safety requirements


Compensation Package:


  • Competitive salary and benefits package
  • Opportunities for professional development and career growth
  • CPD accredited employer
  • A collaborative and supportive work environment
  • Laptop or desktop and work mobile phone provided
  • Flexible working options, dependent on location and role requirements
  • See company website for more information on the benefits of working in EPS Group


We welcome speculative applications from individuals of all levels.

If you are interested in working with us but are not sure if the role above is for you, please feel free to get in touch for an informal discussion about what we do and what you could bring to the team. If you need any reasonable adjustments or have an accessibility request during your recruitment journey, please let our recruiting team know.

Speak to our recruiting team on 022-31200 or email jobs@epswater.com.


Equal Opportunity Statement - EPS Group is an equal opportunities employer.


Company Profile

Since 1968, we have grown from a modest electrical and pumping services business and developed into an innovative, internationally exporting product and service provider, now focused upon the water, wastewater and clean technology sectors, operating across the Republic of Ireland, the UK and Northern Europe.

We are an international water infrastructure specialist, one of the few genuine end-to-end service providers in the global water sector, providing large scale employment, bringing market leading and ground breaking technologies to Ireland and working to improve the country's water infrastructure.

Our vision is to be the best and most rewarding place to work for our teams, to be our customer's outstanding partner of choice and we are committed to being a Net Zero, sustainable business.

title: DevOps Engineer
description:
Company description:

FBD Holdings PLC

Job description:

Talent, Support, Success.

Talent, Support, Success

About the Role

The Release and Environment Management team are looking for a DevOps Engineer to help operate, maintain, and evolve our integration and application platforms. The role focuses on ensuring stable environments, smooth deployments, strong automation practices, and ongoing improvements across both non-production and production landscapes. 

You will work across a range of platform technologies— primarily our IBM WebMethods integration platform—supporting dependable release processes and consistent environment management. This position plays a key role in enhancing our delivery pipelines and ensuring our platforms run efficiently and reliably. 

You will collaborate closely with development, test, operations, and architecture teams to streamline processes, enhance automation, and contribute to the overall maturity of our DevOps practices. Experience working in a regulated or governed environment is beneficial, along with a mindset geared toward continuous improvement and high-quality operational support 

Job Responsibilities
  • Administer and support the IBM WebMethods integration platform (Integration Server, Deployer, Internal/External API Gateway) 
  • Manage the build & release process, and environment configuration across multiple environments. 
  • Build and maintain CI/CD pipelines for the platform, working closely with the development & QA teams. 
  • Provide operational support for the platform – Monitor system performance, manage upgrades, patching, security, and access controls, clustering, performance tuning, troubleshoot incidents, and perform root cause analysis 
  • Strong automation mindset – Continuous improvement and improvements to release processes. 
  • Support occasional application releases or urgent system incidents during evenings and at weekends. 
Education
  • 3+ years experience in a release management, SRE, or DevOps engineer role. 
  • Bachelor’s degree in computer science, Engineering, or a related field. 
  • Certifications in relevant areas such as DevOps, Linux Administration, CI/CD, Azure - desirable. 
Experiences
  • Strong knowledge of the IBM WebMethods platform and the various components supporting it 
  • Demonstrable experience building and deploying containerised applications via Docker and Kubernetes. 
  • Experience with common DevOps tooling (Git, Bash, Python, Ansible, Jira, Bitbucket, Datadog) 
  • Experience administrating and deploying applications in both Windows and Linux (RHEL) environments. 
  • Experience working with and managing CI/CD platforms (Jenkins, Azure DevOps) 
  • Understanding of APIs, REST/SOAP services, and messaging concepts.
  • Cloud experience (Azure)
  • Infrastructure as Code (Terraform)
  • IBM webMethods certifications
  • Java development background 
Competencies
  • Strong troubleshooting and analytical capability 
  • Automation-focused mindset 
  • Ability to work effectively in a team environment and on your own initiative. 
  • Self-starter with a proactive and structured approach to working. 
  • Excellent interpersonal and communication (oral & written) skills.  
  • Ability to present and discuss technical information in a way that establishes rapport, persuades others, and gains understanding. 
  • Excellent process improvement and change management experience. 
  • Excellent organizational, communication and time management skills. 
MCC
  • Excellent communication, negotiation, and stakeholder management skills
  • Strong analytical and problem-solving abilities
  • Ability to work effectively in a fast-paced, dynamic environment
  • Team player
  • Strong interest in continuously improving
Footer

This role sits within the C pay grade in the IT department. 

FBD is proud to be an Equal Opportunity Employer. We welcome applicants of all ages, genders, ethnicities, cultures, religions, languages, sexual orientations, abilities, disabilities, and social backgrounds.

We are dedicated to fostering a diverse, equitable, and inclusive workplace where everyone is encouraged to bring their whole, authentic self to work. Our approach to inclusion is embedded in our hiring practices, pay structures, leadership development, and workplace policies.

At FBD, we believe that when our people feel valued and supported, they thrive, and so does our business. We are proud to foster a culture of collaboration, respect, and shared success at every stage of your career.

Our Benefits Include:
  • Hybrid working – 2 days in the office / 3 days working from home
  • Health Insurance subsidy for employee and family
  • Wellbeing allowance
  • Defined Contribution Pension Scheme
  • Tax Saver Schemes  
  • Lunch Allowance
  • Discounts off home, car and travel insurance.<

title: DevOps Engineer
description:
DevOps Engineer
Location: Dublin (Hybrid - 1 day per week in office)

A fast-growing software product company is seeking a talented DevOps Engineer to join their expanding team. This is an excellent opportunity to work on modern, scalable systems in a collaborative environment where engineering excellence and continuous improvement are key.

This role is ideal for someone who enjoys building reliable cloud infrastructure, driving automation, and working closely with development teams to optimise performance and delivery pipelines.



Key Responsibilities

  • Collaborate with Software Engineering and QA teams to deliver reliable, secure, and scalable solutions
  • Design, build, and maintain CI/CD pipelines to enable efficient software delivery
  • Develop and manage cloud infrastructure using Infrastructure as Code (IaC)
  • Automate operational processes using scripting languages such as Python and Bash
  • Monitor system performance, troubleshoot issues, and ensure high availability
  • Contribute to architectural decisions and continuously improve infrastructure design
  • Participate in incident response and on-call rotations


Key Requirements

  • Strong hands-on experience with AWS (essential) - including services such as EKS, EC2, S3, IAM, RDS, Lambda
  • Proven expertise in Kubernetes (essential) - managing containerised applications at scale
  • Deep experience with Terraform (essential) for Infrastructure as Code
  • 3-5 years' experience in DevOps, SRE, or Platform Engineering roles
  • Strong scripting skills (Python, Bash)
  • Experience with CI/CD tools such as GitHub Actions or GitLab CI/CD
  • Solid troubleshooting skills across infrastructure, deployment, and performance issues
  • Good understanding of cloud security best practices


Nice to Have

  • Experience with modern data platforms (e.g., Snowflake) or workflow tools like Airflow
  • Familiarity with ETL/ELT pipelines and data architecture
  • Experience working in regulated or high-compliance environments


What's on Offer

  • Competitive salary and benefits package
  • Hybrid working model (1 day per week in the Dublin office)
  • Opportunity to work on scalable, high-impact systems
  • Collaborative and supportive engineering culture
  • Strong focus on learning, growth, and continuous improvement

If you're a DevOps Engineer with strong AWS, Kubernetes, and Terraform expertise looking to take the next step in your career, this is a fantastic opportunity to join a high-performing product-focused engineering team.

Apply now to learn more.

Reperio Human Capital acts as an Employment Agency and an Employment Business.

title: Custom Install / Technical Installation Engineer – Galway
description:
About Us

HAS Integration is Ireland’s leading systems integration company, delivering custom-built technology solutions across Audio Visual, Security, Networking, Video Conferencing, and Smart Automation.

Headquartered in Galway, we work on a wide range of commercial and high-end residential projects across Ireland and Europe. With over 22 years of experience, our reputation is built on quality workmanship, technical expertise, and long-term client relationships.

We specialise in custom installs—every project is different, every solution is tailored, and attention to detail matters. We believe attitude, reliability, and pride in your work are just as important as technical experience.

About the Role

We are looking for practical, hands-on Installation Engineers to join our growing project delivery team.

This role would suit someone currently working in:

  • Security systems
  • Alarm or CCTV installation
  • Access control
  • Structured cabling or electrical installation

who is interested in moving into the Custom Install / Smart Technology sector and developing new skills across AV and integrated systems.

No two days are the same—this is a varied, site-based role working on professionally delivered installations.

What You’ll Do

You will be involved in the physical installation and commissioning of integrated technology solutions, including:

  • Installing and routing cables neatly and safely on-site
  • Working with containment, power tools, fixings, and hand tools
  • Terminating and making off data, network, and low-voltage cabling
  • Installing equipment such as displays, speakers, cameras, sensors, control panels, and peripherals
  • Assisting with second-fix terminations and final fit-out
  • Carrying out basic fault finding and testing
  • Working from drawings, plans, and instructions
  • Maintaining tidy, organised rack rooms and headends
  • Collaborating closely with Project Managers and Senior Engineers

Full product and system training will be provided.

What We’re Looking For

We are open to candidates from non‑AV backgrounds who bring strong transferable skills.

Essential qualities:

  • Practical, hands-on experience using power tools and hand tools
  • Experience with cabling, termination, or electrical/security installs
  • Pride in neat, accurate workmanship
  • Willingness to learn new technologies and systems
  • Reliable, punctual, and professional on client sites

Ideally, you will also have:

  • Experience in security, alarm, CCTV, access control, electrical or data installation
  • Ability to follow drawings and technical instructions
  • Basic networking or cabling knowledge
  • A clean, valid Irish driving licence
  • Good communication skills and a positive, team-focused attitude

Why Join HAS Integration?

  • Clear pathway into the Custom Install and Smart Technology industry
  • Training and upskilling across AV, networking, and integrated systems
  • Long-term, secure employment with a well-established company
  • Professional projects, quality clients, and high standards
  • Supportive team environment where effort and attitude are valued

Job Type: Full-time
Location: Site-based, Ireland

Job Types: Full-time, Permanent, Apprenticeship, Graduate

Pay: €33,000.00-€43,000.00 per year

Benefits:

  • Bike to work scheme
  • Company events
  • On-site parking

Ability to commute/relocate:

  • Galway, County Galway: reliably commute or plan to relocate before starting work (required)

Experience:

  • Hands on Installation: 3 years (required)

Work Location: In person

title: Maintenance Technician ( Mechanical or Electrical )
description:
At ABP we aim to hire the brightest and the best. People who are aligned with our values and are passionate about our industry. We are committed to developing our people, whether it be upskilling our employees across a diverse range of skills through our onsite training programs or nurturing the next generation of leaders through our graduate program. No matter what stage of their career our people are at, we empower them to take ownership of their roles.


ABP is one of Europe's leading privately owned agribusiness companies and is the largest beef processor in Ireland and the UK. The company also operates substantial renewable (Olleco), pet food (C&D), and protein divisions. ABP and its affiliates employ over 14,000 people and have over 50 manufacturing plants operating across nine countries. For more information, visit www.abpfoodgroup.com.



Location: ABP Cahir


Responsibilities:

Work with Site Engineering Managers, Planners and API Maintenance Lead on execution of the site maintenance program to deliver compliant, reliable and efficient operation of all plant and equipment.

Develop and lead a Root Cause Failure Analysis Program to reduce general equipment breakdowns and support maintenance personnel to conduct real time Root Cause Analysis.

Work with the Reliability Engineer & Planning to establish and use reliability & work planning metrics/KPI's to monitor improvement and resource optimization e.g. PM/CM ratio, schedule adherence, etc.

To carry out start up checks on machinery throughout the plant on a daily basis.

Ensure that all breakdowns are carried out swiftly and safely at all times.

Ensure that all servicing and maintenance throughout the site is done in an efficient, safe and hygienic manner.

Smooth running of the factory.

Repairs, servicing and good maintenance of machinery.

Work on refrigeration, plumbing and all breakdowns is carried out in a safe and tidy manner.

To implement and follow the preventative maintenance schedule

To ensure that records are completed.

Other duties as assigned.

Refrigeration experience an advantage but not essential

To ensure that the staff comply with the Company Health & Safety policy.

The above job description is designed to give an appreciation of the main functions of the job. It is not intended to be exclusive or exhaustive.


Qualifications

Experience in a fast paced manufacturing environment

Good communication and interpersonal skills, with the ability to take direction

Ability to work well in a team

Ability to challenge others in a professional and constructive manner

Knowledge and experience in a food manufacturing environment an advantage, but not essential

Understanding of Environmental requirements and responsibilities


This is an excellent opportunity for someone seeking a challenging career with one of Europe's leading privately owned agri-business organisations.

title: Site Reliability Engineer
description:

While this background puts us at the top of our game, what really makes us successful is our cutting-edge technology, collaboration between our technologists and traders, and our decision making.As a Site Reliability Engineer at Susquehanna, you’ll be working alongside experienced engineers to solve real problems, being responsible in designing, supporting, maintaining and improving infrastructure across virtual and physical environments, applying a DevOps approach.This role is aimed at graduates and early career professionals passionate about a career in Infrastructure and Site Reliability Engineering. title: DevOps Engineer - IAM Cloud
description:
Optum is a global organization that delivers care, aided by technology to help millions of people live healthier lives. The work you do with our team will directly improve health outcomes by connecting people with the care, pharmacy benefits, data and resources they need to feel their best. Here, you will find a culture guided by diversity and inclusion, talented peers, comprehensive benefits and career development opportunities. Come make an impact on the communities we serve as you help us advance health equity on a global scale. Join us to start Caring. Connecting. Growing together.


IAM Cloud Engineering team at UHG specializes in developing capabilities that enable our cloud enterprise applications and protects access to our cloud assets, data and services.


As a member of this innovative agile team, you will collaborate with others to maintain and optimize these capabilities to secure our growing dynamic cloud computing environments. Engineers on this team will work with many popular vendor and open-source technologies, internally developed applications and cloud native services. You will be challenged to provide technical and thought leadership and to succeed in this role, you need to be eager to learn, able to work through ambiguity, problem solve and communicate effectively.


We are looking for a security-focused DevOps engineer that is a self-starter and demonstrates a solid understanding of the impact of their work. One who is continuously looking for opportunities to simplify and optimize cloud IAM technologies and processes.


Careers with Optum offer flexible work arrangements and individuals who live and work in the Republic of Ireland will have the opportunity to split their monthly work hours between our Dublin or Letterkenny office and telecommuting from a home-based office in a hybrid work model.


What you will do:

  • Define and develop solutions to enforce least privilege access policies
  • Implement and manage access controls, including role-based access control (RBAC)
  • Monitoring, logging, and analyzing system performance using Azure operations suite to maintain system health and optimize costs
  • Develop and implement disaster recovery and data backup strategies and solutions to maintain business continuity
  • Collaborate with development teams to enable CI/CD pipelines for efficient software deployment using Azure services
  • Establish and maintain cloud identity governance frameworks
  • Develop and maintain guardrails to enforce/measure IAM cloud compliance with regulatory requirements
  • Conduct periodic access reviews and monitor user activity
  • Lead cloud migration and modernization initiatives
  • Advise on best practices for cloud security, reliability, and scalability
  • Drive innovation and discovery efforts around new Azure features and services
  • Participates in the development of prototypes and large-scale proof of concepts
  • Maintain knowledgeable around current application security controls


You will be rewarded and recognized for your performance in an environment that will challenge you and give you clear direction on what it takes to succeed in your role, as well as providing development for other roles you may be interested in.

What you will bring:

If you don't meet every single requirement, don't let that hold you back - we're just as excited about potential as we are about qualifications and experience, and you could be exactly who we're looking for.

  • Proven solid hands-on experience in deploying and operating resources in Azure and AWS
  • Demonstrated solid understanding of Infrastructure as Code (IAC) and experience working with CI/CD technologies such as git and Terraform
  • Proven solid hands-on experience with Azure Identity and Access Management concepts and security frameworks
  • Proven solid hands-on experience with Entra ID and Entra Connect Sync
  • Demonstrated solid hands-on experience in developing and managing Organization policies
  • Proven experience with federation technologies and protocols such as SAML, OAuth, OIDC, etc.
  • Proven ample experience operating with cloud platforms, APIs and event-driven automation
  • Demonstrated proficiency with popular scripting and programming language frameworks such as Python, Microsoft Graph PowerShell SDK, Microsoft Graph API, Shell Scripting languages
  • Demonstrated passion for innovation and enjoyment of the challenges of creating something new
  • Proven technical and thought leadership skills, with the ability to collaborate with others to successfully deliver large, complex initiatives
  • Demonstrated solid written and verbal communication skills


Other useful skills and experience include:

  • Certifications in CompTIA Security+, Azure Administrator Associate, Azure Security Engineer Associate, Microsoft Security, Compliance, and Identity Fundamentals, Azure Fundamentals, AWS Cloud Practitioner, AWS Security (Specialty), CCSP or similar
  • Proven previous experience working in/with AWS Identity and Access Management, multi-factor authentication, user provisioning, service principal governance/management, privileged Identity management (PIM), single sign-on (SSO), domain management
  • Proven experience in Cloud IAM functionality across multiple public cloud services (IAM Roles, Permissions and Policies)
  • Demonstrated ability to implement and manage Cloud Infrastructure Entitlement Management (CIEM) to meet organizational access and security objectives
  • Demonstrated advocacy for CI/CD and DevOps best practices
  • Demonstrated fundamental understanding of PHI/PII data and the restrictions around it using limited data

title: Dev Ops Engineer
description:
Acuity Inc. (NYSE: AYI) is a market-leading industrial technology company. We use technology to solve problems in spaces, light and more things to come. Through our two business segments, Acuity Brands Lighting (ABL) and Acuity Intelligent Spaces (AIS), we design, manufacture, and bring to market products and services that make a valuable difference in people’s lives.

We achieve growth through the development of innovative new products and services, including lighting, lighting controls, building management solutions, and an audio, video and control platform. We focus on customer outcomes and drive growth and productivity to increase market share and deliver superior returns. We look to aggressively deploy capital to grow the business and to enter attractive new verticals.

Acuity Inc. is based in Atlanta, Georgia, with operations across North America, Europe and Asia. The Company is powered by approximately 13,000 dedicated and talented associates. Visit us at www.acuityinc.com.

Job Summary

At Acuity, you will join an Agile team focused on building and supporting advanced platforms and applications that drive our business forward.

We are looking for passionate and driven DevOps contributors to join our team. As a DevOps Engineer on the AI Commerce team, you will help architect, automate, and optimize the infrastructure and delivery pipelines that power our AI-driven solutions. You’ll collaborate with a cross functional team to enable rapid, secure, and reliable delivery of innovative products.

This role offers the chance to shape the future of AI commerce by ensuring our platforms are secure, scalable, and efficient. You will contribute to platform improvements, support key initiatives, and make a meaningful impact within a global leader in industrial technology.

Key Tasks & Responsibilities (Essential Functions)

  • Design, implement, and maintain infrastructure and CI/CD pipelines to support the development, testing, and deployment of AI-driven applications.
  • Collaborate with engineering, QA, and data teams to ensure secure, scalable, and efficient delivery of software and services.
  • Promote DevOps and security best practices, automation, and continuous improvement across the team.
  • Monitor, document, and optimize infrastructure, processes, and workflows, staying current with emerging trends and tools in DevOps and cloud technologies.

Preferred Skills and Experience

  • Bachelor’s degree in Computer Science, Engineering, or related field, or equivalent experience.
  • 4+ years in a software development environment, with at least 3 years in DevOps, Platform, and/or Site Reliability Engineering.
  • Programming and scripting skills in Python, with experience in automation and infrastructure tooling.
  • Deep experience with CI/CD pipeline design and maintenance (GitHub Actions, Azure DevOps, Jenkins).
  • Proven expertise with major cloud providers, with preference for Azure.
  • Strong background in Linux and familiarity with development workflows and tooling.
  • Experience with containerization (Docker) and orchestration (Kubernetes, Helm, Kustomize, Kyverno, CNI, etc.).
  • Hands-on experience with Infrastructure as Code eg Bicep (preferred), Terraform, Ansible, etc.
  • Familiarity with security best practices for CI/CD pipelines, container security, and secrets management.
  • Experience integrating automated testing and security scanning (Snyk, Bandit) into DevOps workflows.
  • Experience with monitoring and logging stacks, including Azure Monitor, Application Insights, Grafana, Prometheus, Elasticsearch, etc.
  • Excellent problem-solving, debugging, and analytical skills.
  • Strong communication skills and ability to collaborate across Agile, cross-functional teams.
  • Experience with collaboration and documentation tools (JIRA, Confluence, Miro, Teams).

Nice to have:

  • Experience supporting AI and ML workloads, data pipelines, and Python-based applications in production.
  • Hands-on experience with enterprise-scale CI/CD for microservice-based cloud applications.
  • Familiarity with event-driven architecture and secure software development practices.

We value diversity and are an equal opportunity employer. All qualified applicants will be considered for employment without regards to race, color, age, gender, sexual orientation, gender identity and expression, ethnicity or national origin, disability, pregnancy, religion, covered veteran status, protected genetic information, or any other characteristic protected by law.


Accommodation for Applicants with Disabilities: As an equal opportunity employer, Acuity Inc. is committed to providing reasonable accommodations in its application process for qualified individuals with disabilities and disabled veterans. If you have difficulty using our online system due to a disability and need an accommodation, you may contact us at (770) 922-9000, select option 4. Please clearly indicate what type of accommodation you are requesting and for what requisition.


Any unsolicited resumes sent to Acuity Inc. from a third party, such as an Agency recruiter, including unsolicited resumes sent to an Acuity Inc. mailing address, fax machine or email address, directly to Acuity Inc. employees, or to Acuity Inc. resume database will be considered Acuity Inc. property. Acuity Inc. will NOT pay a fee for any placement resulting from the receipt of an unsolicited resume.

title: M365 & Power Platform Engineer
description:
M365 & Power Platform EngineerOverview:Do you want a chance to really make a difference?We are building software to really make the world a greener place. Countries using our services increase their ability to effectively utilise renewable sources of energy.As a Power Platform Engineer, you will work with the Enterprise Technology team, which is responsible for the AWS Cloud, IT, Cyber Security, MS Power Platform Cloud, and internal support domains. You will design and implement solutions which will drive VIOTAS’ global expansion.About VIOTAS:Established in Limerick, Ireland in 2013, VIOTAS set about designing and delivering new innovations to address the challenges posed by increased renewable generation in electrical grids: How can a grid be balanced when a large amount of electricity is coming from unpredictable renewable sources that are difficult to forecast and that change the fundamental principles of how grids operate?In response, VIOTAS developed proprietary technologies that place electricity customers at the core of the solution. By managing their electricity consumption and onsite generation in real-time, VIOTAS help balance the wider electricity grid. The company then aggregates participating customers to form Virtual Power Plants which are traded in the electricity market, competing against conventional fossil fuel power stations. This approach not only generates significant revenue for participating customers but also contributes to reducing carbon emissions. With a global expansion strategy, VIOTAS, currently with over 110 staff, operates in Ireland, Poland, Texas and Australia.We are a highly motivated team that are pushing boundaries to achieve success. title: Scaled Customer Engineer, SMB, Google Cloud
description:
Minimum qualifications:

  • Bachelor's degree or equivalent practical experience.
  • 4 years of experience with cloud infrastructure engineering, on-premise infrastructure engineering, virtualization, or containerization platforms.
  • Experience delivering technical presentations.

Preferred qualifications:

  • MBA or Master's degree in Computer Science or a related field.
  • Certification in GCP (e.g., GCP Professional Cloud Architect/Associate Customer Engineer).
  • Experience with WAN/internet-related technologies and architectures across SaaS, PaaS, IaaS, and cloud productivity suites.
  • Experience architecting and developing software or infrastructure for distributed systems and with application development technologies.
  • Experience in data and AI solutions and environments.
  • Experience with relational databases, NoSQL databases, or big data technologies and containers.

About the job

The Google Cloud Platform team helps customers transform and build what's next for their business, all with technology built in the cloud. Our products are engineered for security, reliability, and scalability, running the full stack from infrastructure to applications to devices and hardware. Our teams are dedicated to helping our customers, developers, small and large businesses, educational institutions, and government agencies see the benefits of our technology come to life.

In this role, you will understand the needs of our customers and help shape the future of businesses of all sizes use technology to connect with customers, employees, and partners.Google Cloud accelerates every organization’s ability to digitally transform its business and industry. We deliver enterprise-grade solutions that leverage Google’s cutting-edge technology, and tools that help developers build more sustainably. Customers in more than 200 countries and territories turn to Google Cloud as their trusted partner to enable growth and solve their most critical business problems.

Responsibilities

  • Work with the team to identify and qualify business opportunities, understand customer technical objections, and develop the strategy to resolve technical blockers.
  • Share enterprise infrastructure modernization to support the technical relationship with customers, including technology advocacy, supporting bid responses, product and solution briefings, proof-of-concept work, and partnering with product management to prioritize solutions impacting customer adoption to Google Cloud.
  • Work with Google Cloud products to demonstrate and prototype integrations in customer and partner environments.
  • Guide customers through assessments of existing legacy application environments and provide recommendations on a prioritization roadmap for infrastructure and application modernization.
  • Recommend integration strategies, enterprise architectures, platforms, and application infrastructure required to implement a solution on Google Cloud.
Google is proud to be an equal opportunity workplace and is an affirmative action employer. We are committed to equal employment opportunity regardless of race, color, ancestry, religion, sex, national origin, sexual orientation, age, citizenship, marital status, disability, gender identity or Veteran status. We also consider qualified applicants regardless of criminal histories, consistent with legal requirements. See also Google's EEO Policy and EEO is the Law. If you have a disability or special need that requires accommodation, please let us know by completing our Accommodations for Applicants form.

title: Microsoft Technology Engineer
description:
We are seeking a talented and proactive Microsoft Technical Engineer to join our team. This role will be responsible for supporting, maintaining, administering, and enhancing the organisation's Microsoft technology stack across SharePoint, Dynamics 365 CRM, and the Microsoft Power Platform.


The role blends technical administration, solution development, and business engagement, enabling users and teams to work more effectively through secure, scalable, and well-designed Microsoft solutions. A strong focus will be placed on low-code / no-code development, continuous improvement, and automation.


Reporting to the IT Service Deliver Manager, you will contribute to the support & development of SharePoint & MS Dynamics customisations, while also supporting the rollout, governance, and optimisation of Microsoft 365 and Power Platform technologies. This role is ideal for someone who enjoys cloud engineering, thrives in a collaborative environment, and is passionate about modern Microsoft technologies.


Key Responsibilities


Microsoft Cloud, SharePoint, Dynamics & Power Platform


  • Administer and maintain Microsoft 365, MS Dynamics, SharePoint Online, Power Platform, and Azurebased solutions, including configuration, user management, and system optimisation.
  • Lead or support the design, configuration, and rollout of SharePoint Online sites, intranet, departmental sites, and collaboration spaces.
  • Manage SharePoint information architecture, permissions, and governance frameworks.
  • Develop automated workflows using Power Automate and integrate them with existing systems.
  • Support the rollout and adoption of integrated technologies such as Copilot and Power BI.
  • Perform SharePoint maintenance activities including backups, audits, and optimisation.
  • Provide user support, training, and guidance on SharePoint and Power Platform tools.
  • Track usage, generate performance reports, and drive continuous improvement.
  • Regularly audit permissions to ensure access is appropriately governed, minimising the risk of oversharing and unintended access.
  • Stay current with emerging Microsoft technologies and best practices.


Collaboration & Continuous Improvement

  • Work closely with internal customers to clarify requirements and deliver effective solutions.
  • Collaborate with crossfunctional teams to drive automation and digital transformation.
  • Keep up to date with best practices in Microsoft development and cloud technologies.
  • Support other IT team members to meet departmental service and quality objectives.


Summary of Requirements

Technical Skills

  • Experience of implementing security best practices for managing access and governance in MS Technology Solutions (SharePoint/Teams, MS Dynamics, Power Platform).
  • Administration and configuration of SharePoint sites, libraries, permissions, and workflows.
  • Administration and configuration of Microsoft Dynamics 365 architecture, including entities, relationships, security roles, and environments.
  • Working knowledge of Power Automate for building automated workflows.
  • Familiarity with SharePoint Online administration tools and capabilities.
  • Integration of SharePoint, MS Dynamics, and Power Automate with other systems (advantageous).
  • Knowledge of entire Microsoft stack including Microsoft Purview a distinct advantage.


Experience

  • 3-5 years' experience administering and supporting Microsoft 365, including SharePoint Online, Microsoft Teams, and OneDrive.
  • 2-4 years' hands-on experience administering and configuring Microsoft Dynamics 365, including entities (Dataverse tables), security roles, forms, views, and workflows.
  • 2-4 years' experience designing and delivering Power Platform solutions, including Power Apps, Power Automate, and Dataverse, in a low-code / no-code environment.
  • Demonstrated experience auditing, managing, and governing access and permissions across Microsoft platforms to ensure secure and controlled information sharing.
  • Proven experience working directly with business stakeholders to gather requirements, deliver solutions, and support adoption in a production environment.
  • Strong experience with Microsoft technologies including Azure, Intune, MS365.


Additional Skills

  • Strong communication and interpersonal skills, including the ability to train and support internal users.
  • Ability to analyse business needs and translate them into actionable technical solutions.
  • Strong problemsolving and analytical skills with the ability to troubleshoot and optimise performance.
  • Commitment to continuous learning and staying updated with Microsoft technologies.


Desirable Skills

  • Microsoft specialist certifications relating to Power Platform, Dynamics 365, Microsoft 365, or Azure are highly desirable, with support provided for ongoing professional development.
  • Experience with Teams, OneDrive, or other Microsoft 365 tools.
  • Microsoft Security, Compliance, and Identity Fundamentals certification.
  • Microsoft Power Platform Developer Associate or Microsoft Power Platform Solution Architect Expert.
