title: 2026 Graduate Programme - Earth & Environment - Mining
description:
What if you could do the kind of work the world needs?

At WSP, you can access our global scale, contribute to landmark projects and connect with the brightest minds in your field to do the best work of your life. You can embrace your curiosity in a culture that celebrates new ideas and diverse perspectives. You can experience a world of opportunity and the chance to shape a career as unique as you.

Our mining specialists work on an array of projects in the mining industry covering all regions globally. Work includes high level Due Diligence Reviews to detailed Feasibility Studies and detailed design of mines and mining infrastructure covering Geology & Resource Estimation, Mining, Geotechnical and Geo-mechanical Engineering. Hydrology and Hydrogeology, Geochemistry, Tailings and Waste Management for mines. Heap Leach Design, Mine Closure Studies, Financial Assessments and Construction Supervision and QA/QC Services.

We are looking for Graduates to join our Mine Waste team who have a flexible approach and an appetite to learn in a leading Engineering and Professional Advisory services consultancy. We want you to enjoy working in a collaborative environment where you can apply your skills working for a diverse range of clients and projects.

We are recruiting for Graduates in the following teams and locations:

  • Mine Waste Team: Naas, Ireland

A little bit more about your role and the team…

Our UK and Ireland Mine Waste team works on a range of projects situated across the Nordics, Eastern Europe and Africa. At WSP, we understand that responsible mining includes the effective management of all types of mine waste. We provide advice and develop tailings and waste management approaches that align with evolving standards and best practices throughout the entire mining life cycle. Our technical expertise, global governance programs, and deep understanding of risk enable us to create innovative solutions. By integrating hazard identification, risk assessment, and control methods early in the design process, we mitigate health and safety risks and safeguard communities and the environment.

Leveraging our global network, you will work with bespoke teams with diverse skill sets to meet the unique requirements of each project. Our team takes a holistic view, incorporating ESG principles at every phase of a mine and facility.

From innovative tailings storage solutions to advanced dewatering techniques and resilient designs, our multi-disciplinary team advances responsible mining practices through integrated mine waste solutions.

Our Mining Teams:

Mine Waste:

As a Graduate Geotechnical / Mine Waste Engineer, you will help shape the safe and sustainable management of mine waste facilities across the Nordics, Eastern Europe and Africa. From tailings storage systems to geotechnical investigations, this role places you at the forefront of solutions that protect communities and the environment.

Day-to-day activities could include:

  • Working as part of a diverse team of engineers, scientists and modellers from conceptual design through to detail design and engineering
  • Providing inputs input to design focused on tailings management facilities, heap leach facilities, water impoundments and waste rock dump design, soil mechanics, soil improvement methods, geosynthetic materials engineering and related civil engineering work for projects worldwide
  • Assisting project managers in the successful delivery of projects by managing own involvement in projects, including time, budget and quality control
  • Preparing technical reports, drawings, specifications, bill of quantities, and cost estimates
  • Exciting opportunities to travel within the UK and Internationally to assist clients in solving engineering problems and support construction of WSP designs. Travel outside of UK will require overnight stays, and likely duration will depend on the nature of the project/client needs and project location
  • Developing and maintaining good relationships with new and existing clients and be able to effectively represent WSP on site
  • Liaising with the Mining Team networks, which include specialists within WSP both nationally and internationally.

You will join an enthusiastic and supportive team, providing you with a challenging and fulfilling career. In this team, you will be dealing with internal and external stakeholders (including regulatory authorities), collaborating with colleagues and adapting to dynamic project environments.

Graduate Development Programme and Future progression

Our two-year Graduate Development programme is designed to support personal and professional development and growth through an engaging blended learning experience. The programmes include core business and professional skills training, career development workshops and resources, valuable networking opportunities, mentoring for professional registration and a breadth of on-the-job experience.

To find out more, please visit: Who we are - ECP

Our teams are dedicated to supporting your career growth from the very beginning. Many employees have started as graduates and successfully advanced within the company. We encourage you to achieve chartered status with professional bodies, ensuring your development is a priority.

To find out more about Sectors and Specialisms we recruit our Graduates into, as well as qualification requirements, please click on the graduate website or graduate brochure link below.

What will we be looking for you to demonstrate

You’ll have graduated with at least a 2:2 in a degree in Geotechnical or Civil Engineering. Please visit our Graduate brochure or Graduate website or for further information.

  • Candidates must complete the application form in full answering

title: Geotechnical Engineer
description:
Ground Engineering

Europe Region

DUB0000VF


Geotechnical Engineer

Dublin or Cork | Ireland

Shape a future with purpose at Arup in Ireland.

Engineer the ground beneath tomorrow’s most critical infrastructure.

Arup’s purpose, shared values and collaborative approach have set us apart for over 75 years, guiding how we shape a better world.

Our Ground Engineering team is the largest in Ireland, bringing together geotechnical engineers, hydrogeologists, contaminated land specialists, and engineering geologists. We deliver innovative projects with creativity and passion, shaping the built environment across Ireland and the wider European business.

The Opportunity

This is an excellent opportunity to join a highly specialised team and contribute to a dynamic portfolio of complex, multidisciplinary projects. You will play a key role in shaping some of the most significant infrastructure and development schemes in Ireland and across Europe.

While the role focuses on geotechnical engineering design and delivery, you will also gain valuable exposure to the full breadth of Ground Engineering disciplines. This position offers a stimulating and supportive environment to advance your technical, business, and people skills, working alongside industry experts and developing relationships with our firm’s wider engineering community. It also offers the opportunity to place sustainability at the heart of engineering solutions.

As a Geotechnical Engineer at Arup, you will:

  • Deliver geotechnical design and analysis for foundations, retaining structures, slope stability, and earthworks on major international commissions (e.g., hyperscale data centres in Europe, the Fehmarnsund Link) and nationally significant Irish infrastructure (e.g., Metrolink, DART+, N6 Galway Ring Road).

  • Prepare high-quality design reports, technical specifications, desk studies, and site walkovers for educational, pharmaceutical, industrial, and commercial developments.

  • Plan, supervise, and interpret geotechnical ground investigations, providing technical support during the construction phase, including periodic site visits.

  • Incorporate the latest digital tools, automation, and numerical analysis into our projects to enhance project delivery.

  • Develop your project management and commercial awareness skills, while supporting and mentoring junior colleagues as you strengthen your own leadership capabilities.

Does this role inspire you?

We're looking for a motivated and ambitious engineer looking to build a strong foundation in geotechnical design. We also value:

  • An honours Bachelor’s degree in Civil, Geotechnical, or Structural Engineering (or equivalent). A post-graduate qualification in Geotechnical Engineering or Soil Mechanics is an advantage.
  • Over 3 years of post-graduate experience in geotechnical design and/or construction.
  • Strong technical understanding of the design of deep excavations, deep and shallow foundations, earthworks, slope stability, and soft ground solutions.
  • Good planning, organisational, and time management skills, with the ability to maintain personal effectiveness across several projects and communicate confidently in both technical and non-technical settings.
  • Experience in planning, managing, supervising, and interpreting ground investigation contracts, and/or experience in numerical analysis and GIS.
  • A commitment to continuous professional development, ideally working towards Chartered status, with the flexibility to travel and work on-site (a full driver’s licence is a strong advantage).

Why Arup?

At Arup, we believe in rewarding our members for their contributions to our shared success. As a trust-owned company, we have the freedom to pursue meaningful work that aligns with our purpose and values. We also offer a unique profit share program, allowing our members to share in the results of our collective efforts.

Benefits that work for you:

  • Professional Growth and Development: Benefit from continuous learning opportunities, training programs, and mentorship to enhance your skills and advance your career at Arup.


  • Global Opportunities:     Collaborate with colleagues worldwide and explore potential short-term or long-term assignments at other Arup offices and project locations.

  • Financial Well-being and Ownership: Receive a competitive salary and benefits package and share in Arup's success through our global profit-sharing programme.

  • Wellbeing and Culture: At Arup we are committed to fostering a healthy working culture by offering a range of health and wellbeing benefits, including health insurance, flexible working schemes, wellness programmes and an Employee Assistance Programme.

  • Equality, Diversity and Inclusion: We strive to embed inclusion in our way of working, our interactions with one another and the projects we deliver. We are committed to creating an inclusive working environment for talented people of any background, based on merit, fairness and respect.

At Arup, you belong to an extraordinary collective where individuality is encouraged. Our strength comes from how we respect, share, and connect our diverse experiences, perspectives, and ideas. You'll have the opportunity to do socially useful work that has meaning – to Arup, to your career, and to the clients and communities we serve.

We are proud to be an equal oppo

title: Experienced Power System Engineer
description:
At Premium Power we challenge and develop our engineers to meet their true capability.

We are currently looking for Experienced Power Systems Engineers who wish to expand their engineering knowledge and are keen to undertake new challenges.

About us

Premium Power is on a mission to provide best in class electrical engineering consulting on a global scale. We specialize in power systems, electrical safety and arc flash and we collaborate with an outstanding list of household names including Dell, Pfizer, GE and many more.

Our ambition is to be the leader in the provision of technology services for electrical energy systems and the optimization of power systems. We’ve made some great strides already and we’ve a lot more to do. Growing our team, we’re looking for a Power Systems Engineer to join us.

Premium Power is now an ABB company, forming part of ABB’s Electrification Service division. This marks an exciting new chapter for our team and significantly expands the opportunities available to our engineers. ABB is a global technology leader in electrification and automation, helping industries run high-performance operations while improving energy efficiency and sustainability.

As part of ABB Electrification Service, Premium Power combines our deep expertise in power systems engineering with ABB’s global reach, advanced technologies, and innovation ecosystem. For our team and future graduates, this means access to world-class projects, cutting-edge tools, and the chance to contribute to the transformation of power systems at a global scale.

Purpose of the Role:

  • To work as a power engineer on a range of exciting projects for global clients in a rewarding team environment.
  • To develop technical and solution development skills to develop into an industry recognised consulting engineer.
  • The position would suit a practical and adaptable individual who wishes to work in a progressive innovative electrical consultancy.

Key Duties and Responsibilities:

  • Provide technical input and leadership on a variety of complex projects.
  • Work closely with and interpret client requirements and translate into deliverables.
  • Ensure project outputs meet client satisfaction, time and budgetary requirements.
  • Conduct in-depth power systems analysis.
  • Prepare detailed technical and non-technical reports.
  • Attend client meetings remotely, manage expectations through effective communications and timely responsiveness.
  • Track and report on project performance and client satisfaction.
  • Mentor, coach and develop more junior engineers. On-line training to be delivered where applicable.
  • Become familiar with the company’s quality system and try to enhance where possible.
  • Have an active role in internal workshops and groups that benefit the company’s growth.
  • Be actively involved in the company’s sales strategies, with previous experience and ideas welcome for enhancing the companies international portfolio.

Typical projects include:

  • Strategic power system analysis: redundancy, short circuit, power flow, protection coordination
  • Electrical safety analysis: arc flash and electrical risk assessments.
  • Grid connection and embedded generation connections analysis: static & dynamic transient stability, harmonics, flicker, transformer inrush.
  • Power quality evaluation: earthing, harmonics, sag ride-through.
  • Electrical systems reliability assessments.
  • Software based analysis solutions.
  • Supporting wind project and installation teams.
  • Emerging technology reviews.
  • Failure investigations and advice.

Experience and Qualifications:

  • Qualified Electrical Engineer with 3+ years of previous power systems study experience including short circuit, protection coordination and arc flash.
  • Demonstrated experience with Power System Study software (SKM, EasyPower, ETAP, or similar).
  • Experience in designing unit protection settings e.g. Bus and transformer differential 87B & 87T, Restricted Earth Fault, and Directional Protection 67.
  • Ability to carry out cable sizing calculations according to the relevant standards such as IEC 60502-2 and IEC60364-5-52.
  • Experience with Power quality and harmonic analysis would be favourable.
  • Expert on design, calculations, and specifications associated with power system protection.
  • Proven application of protection coordination studies.
  • Superior relationship building and stakeholder management.
  • High performance with a love of learning.
  • Fluent in English with excellent communication, written and verbal.

What we can offer

  • Excellent career development, award winning projects and an excellent remuneration package.
  • High commitment by management to training and career development
  • Premium Power employees work on interesting projects in a supportive and friendly environment, whilst earning market leading salaries.
  • Premium Power offers the opportunity to work within an exciting and growing sector on a diverse range of technical, strategic and regulatory consultancy projects for various industries: Data Centres, Pharma, Medical, Semi Conductor, Food & Beverage, Heavy Industry, etc.
  • Discretionary annual performance-based bonus
  • Flexible working hours
  • Generous Annual leave entitlements
  • Company contributory pension scheme
  • Generous expenses and laptop
  • Paid membership fees for one professional institute
  • Excellent CPD programme
  • Health Insurance

Work Locations

You’ll have the flexibility to work from either of our offices:

  • Balbrigg

title: Platform Operations Specialist - Systems Infrastructure and Excellence - T&S
description: ResponsibilitiesThe Systems Infrastructure and Excellence (SIE) team plays a critical role in enhancing the operational capabilities and infrastructure of the Trust & Safety (T&S) organization. We focus on optimizing and automating workflows for T&S systems to reduce operational costs and drive process excellence through strategic roadmapping and business prioritization. Our core functions include building robust tooling governance that aligns with compliance requirements and removing tooling fragmentation to create a more cohesive environment. We also empower moderation efficiency and quality with AI applications and collaborate with Operations, Engineering and Product teams to deliver user-centric solutions. Additionally, we build common frameworks to integrate both human and machine moderation and deliver advanced business analytics to provide actionable insights.Responsibilities:- User and Business Insight: Deeply understand business requirements by analyzing user feedback, conducting user research, and leveraging data to identify pain points and user needs.- Data Strategy: Define and manage the data index system, identify key metrics, and drive data infrastructure improvements to support business optimization.- Product Implementation: Translate business requirements into clear product documentation (MRD/BRD), develop platform and tool-based solutions, and coordinate with R&D teams to ensure successful implementation.- Project Management: Proactively track and manage the progress of platform requirements, from testing and verification to launch and promotion with the business, to ensure timely and high-quality delivery.- User Enablement: Create and maintain platform operation documents, lead training sessions, and promote new features to boost user adoption and product understanding.- Process Optimization: Continuously identify and improve and automate operational processes to enhance efficiency and effectiveness across the product development lifecycle.- Cross-Functional Collaboration: Serve as the primary liaison for cross-functional teams (including R&D, business, and legal), respond to urgent issues, and coordinate resources to ensure the smooth execution of global projects.QualificationsMinimum Qualifications - Bachelor’s degree in Computer Science, Engineering, Business, Product Management, or a related technical/analytical field - within 3 years of graduation- Foundational understanding or experience with AI, LLMs, agents, and AI infrastructure. - Strong ability to quickly understand complex business contexts, operational logic, and system architecture. - Data-driven mindset with the ability to leverage insights for business optimization. - Exceptional logical thinking, problem-solving, and business abstraction skills. - Strong communication skills with the ability to collaborate effectively across product, business, and R&D teams. Preferred Qualifications:- Internship, placement year, early-career, or professional experience in product, project, operations, or technical program management. - Exposure to technical product development, automation systems, or AI-driven business processes. - Experience working cross-functionally in fast-paced, global environments. - Additional relevant industry experience beyond early-career stage is considered a plus.Trust & SafetyContent that this role interacts with includes images, video, and text related to every-day life, but it can also include (but is not limited to) bullying; hate speech; child safety; depictions of harm to self and others, and harm to animals. Hence, it is possible that this role will be exposed to harmful content on a daily basis. ​​TikTok recognises that keeping our platform safe for the TikTok communities is no ordinary job which can be both rewarding and psychologically demanding and emotionally taxing for some. This is why we are sharing the potential hazards, risks and implications in this unique line of work from the start, so our candidates are well informed before joining. ​​We are committed to the wellbeing of all our employees and promise to provide comprehensive and evidence-based programs, to promote and support physical and mental wellbeing throughout each employee's journey with us. We believe that wellbeing is a relationship and that everyone has a part to play, so we work in collaboration and consultation with our employees and across our functions in order to ensure a truly person-centred, innovative and integrated approach.​About TikTokTikTok is the leading destination for short-form mobile video. At TikTok, our mission is to inspire creativity and bring joy. TikTok's global headquarters are in Los Angeles and Singapore, and we also have offices in New York City, London, Dublin, Paris, Berlin, Dubai, Jakarta, Seoul, and Tokyo.​Why Join UsInspiring creativity is at the core of TikTok's mission. Our innovative product is built to help people authentically express themselves, discover and connect – and our global, diverse teams make that possible. Together, we create value for our communities, inspire creativity and bring joy - a mission we work towards every day.​We strive to do great things with great people. We lead with curiosity, humility, and a desire to make impact in a rapidly growing tech company. Every challenge is an opportunity to learn and innovate as one team. We're resilient and embrace challenges as they come. By constantly iterating and fostering an "Always Day 1" mindset, we achieve meaningful breakthroughs for ourselves, our company, and our users. When we create and grow together, the possibilities are limitless. Join us.​​Diversity & Inclusion​TikTok is committed to creating an inclusive space where employees are valued for their skills, experiences, and unique perspectives. Our platform connects people from across the globe and so does our workplace. At TikTok, our mission is to inspire creativity and bring joy. To achieve that goal, we are committed to celebrating our diverse voice

title: Graduate Electronics Engineer (2026)
description:
About MBRYONICS

MBRYONICS is at the forefront of free space optical communications. Our product portfolio covers a wide range of emerging technologies from photonic integrated circuits to advanced optical systems and embedded software solutions. Our optical communications systems are integrated and deployed in satellite communications systems. The work is exciting and challenging and is at the forefront of the technology domains. As part of our expansion, we are currently looking for a suitably motivated - Graduate Electronics - to join our group (Starting Summer 2026)


We are looking for a motivated Graduate Electronics Engineer to join our team., this is a unique opportunity to kickstart your career at the forefront of space technology, contributing to the design and delivery of space-grade hardware and next-generation Free Space Optics (FSO).

We offer an environment where your contributions have an immediate, visible impact. You won't just be a small cog in a large machine; you will be mentored by industry experts and given real responsibility from day one, working on complex systems destined for space.


What you'll do

  • Design and schematic capture of circuits for optical control, electromechanical control, optical readout and optical signal chain processing
  • Printed Circuit Board (PCB) layout of above designed circuits, including exposure to high speed RF layout
  • Support component selection suitable for space-grade hardware and aerospace deployment
  • Design of circuits to AS/EN9100 and relevant JEDEC standards, with specific reference to aerospace standards
  • Creation and specification of power budgets for (sub)systems
  • Development of (sub)systems from prototype to aerospace-qualified products
  • Work closely with interdisciplinary mechanical and optical teams to design in mechanically and thermally constrained environments with strict optical requirements
  • Design and modelling of analogue (optical) readout & signal processing circuits such as TIAs, DACs, etc.
  • Simulation of PCBs for next generation Free Space Optics (FSO) transceivers
  • Development of noise models and link budgets
  • Test of designed circuits to validate performance in a laboratory environment
  • Documentation and review of design, test & validation of (sub)system designs
  • Design of test platforms for circuit testing

Who you are

Bachelors or Masters in Electronic Engineering or similar


  • Strong fundamental understanding of electronics design, including analogue and digital circuit design
  • Familiarity with PCB design software: Altium, EAGLE, KiCAD, or PADs
  • Foundational knowledge of signal integrity and impedance matching
  • Experience with SPICE simulation for analogue circuit verification
  • Understanding of communications protocols e.g. CAN, SPI, UART, I2C
  • Hands-on experience with typical electronic laboratory test equipment (oscilloscopes, multimeters, etc.) through university projects or internships would be beneficial.

Desirable

  • Internship or project experience in aerospace, defence, or high-reliability sectors
  • Interest in radiation-hard component selection and aerospace environmental constraints
  • Experience with high-speed PCB layout or RF design principles
  • Familiarity with thermal modelling or electrical power budgeting
  • Knowledge of aerospace electronic design standards (e.g. AS/EN 9100)


What's in it for you

Career Growth: As a scaleup, MBRYONICS offers a high-impact environment where you will take on real responsibility early. Develop your technical and leadership skills while contributing to a world-class space-age communication program.

Starting Salary of €44k

Space-Grade Development: Gain hands-on experience developing hardware destined for orbit, working on the next generation of Free Space Optics.

Collaboration: Work with talented professionals across disciplines to drive impactful solutions in a fast-paced, agile setting.

Innovation: Be part of a team pioneering advancements in manufacturing engineering for cutting-edge FSO systems.

Work-Life Balance: Enjoy flexible work schedules and a comprehensive benefits package.

If you're ready to launch your career and drive the future of space-age communication, apply now and join the MBRYONICS team as our Graduate Electronics Engineer.


Diversity, Inclusion and Equal Opportunities

Core ethical values, diversity and inclusion are invaluable to MBRYONICS’ business success. We recognise that the broad perspectives that result from recruiting, developing and retaining a globally diverse workforce promotes innovation and helps us succeed as a business enterprise. It is the policy and practice of the Company to provide equal opportunities to all employees and applicants. The Company will not tolerate discrimination on the basis of gender, race, religion, sexual orientation, gender identity, civil status, family status, disability, age, or membership of the Traveller community.

title: Rust Developer - Remote
description: Job Title: Rust EngineerJob Type: ContractorLocation: RemoteRole DescriptionAs an hourly paid, fully remote Rust Engineer for AI Data Training, you will review AI-generated Rust code and explanations or generate your own, evaluate the reasoning quality and step-by-step problem-solving, and provide expert feedback that helps models produce answers that are accurate, logical, and clearly explained. You will assess solutions for correctness, safety, and adherence to the prompt; identify errors in ownership, borrowing, lifetimes, or algorithmic reasoning; fact-check information; write high-quality explanations and model solutions that demonstrate idiomatic Rust patterns; and rate and compare multiple AI responses based on correctness and reasoning quality. This role is with SME Careers, a fast-growing AI Data Services company and subsidiary of SuperAnnotate that provides AI training data for many of the world’s largest AI companies and foundation model labs. Your work will directly help improve the world’s premier AI models while giving you the flexibility of impactful, detail-oriented remote contract work.Your Profile1-2+ years of professional Rust development experience in backend, CLI, or systems-focused projects.Strong understanding of Rust’s ownership, borrowing, and lifetime model, with the ability to reason clearly about aliasing and data races.Solid software engineering experience in at least one of backend services, command-line tools, or systems programming using Rust.Ability to evaluate safe, idiomatic Rust code, including appropriate use of traits, generics, pattern matching, and error handling.Significant experience using LLMs or AI coding assistants while programming, combined with a disciplined approach to validating their output.Excellent English writing skills, capable of producing precise, structured, and pedagogical technical explanations.Minimum Bachelor’s degree in Computer Science or a closely related technical field.Previous experience with AI data training, code review at scale, or evaluation of AI-generated technical content is strongly preferred; Minimum C1 English proficiency and an extremely detail-oriented working style are required.Highly preferred: experience with Tokio, Actix, Serde, and async Rust patterns in production or advanced side projects.Preferred: competitive programming experience, contributions to Rust open-source ecosystems, and demonstrated ability to reason about performance, concurrency, and correctness in Rust code.Key ResponsibilitiesDevelop AI Training Content: Create detailed prompts in various topics and responses to guide AI learning, ensuring the models reflect a comprehensive understanding of diverse subjects.Optimize AI Performance: Evaluate and rank AI responses to enhance the models accuracy, fluency, and contextual relevance.Ensure Model Integrity: Test AI models for potential inaccuracies or biases, validating their reliability across use cases.

title: Software Engineer III, Google Compute Engine, Fleet Management
description:
Minimum qualifications:
  • Bachelor's degree in Computer Science or equivalent practical experience.
  • 2 years of experience with software development in C++, Java, Python, or Go programming languages.

Preferred qualifications:
  • Master's degree in Computer Science or a related technical field.
  • 2 years of experience with data structures and algorithms.
  • Experience with cloud computing platforms (i.e., Google Cloud Platform).
  • Knowledge of C++ and Go.

About the job

Google Cloud's software engineers develop the next-generation technologies that change how billions of users connect, explore, and interact with information and one another. We're looking for engineers who bring fresh ideas from all areas, including information retrieval, distributed computing, large-scale system design, networking and data storage, security, artificial intelligence, natural language processing, UI design and mobile; the list goes on and is growing every day. As a software engineer, you will work on a specific project critical to Google Cloud's needs with opportunities to switch teams and projects as you and our fast-paced business grow and evolve. You will anticipate our customer needs and be empowered to act like an owner, take action and innovate. We need our engineers to be versatile, display leadership qualities and be enthusiastic to take on new problems across the full-stack as we continue to push technology forward.

Google Cloud accelerates every organization's ability to digitally transform its business and industry. We deliver enterprise-grade solutions that leverage Google's cutting-edge technology, and tools that help developers build more sustainably. Customers in more than 200 countries and territories turn to Google Cloud as their trusted partner to enable growth and solve their most critical business problems.

title: Graduate Robotic Systems Solutions (RSS) Systems Engineer
description:
Come join Analog Devices (ADI) – a place where Innovation meets Impact. For more than 55 years, Analog Devices has been inventing new breakthrough technologies that transform lives. At ADI you will work alongside the brightest minds to collaborate on solving complex problems that matter from autonomous vehicles, drones and factories to augmented reality and remote healthcare.

ADI fosters a culture that focuses on employees through beneficial programs, aligned goals, continuous learning opportunities, and practices that create a more sustainable future.

About Analog Devices

Analog Devices, Inc. (NASDAQ: ADI ) is a global semiconductor leader that bridges the physical and digital worlds to enable breakthroughs at the Intelligent Edge. ADI combines analog, digital, and software technologies into solutions that help drive advancements in digitized factories, mobility, and digital healthcare, combat climate change, and reliably connect humans and the world. With revenue of more than $9 billion in FY24 and approximately 24,000 people globally, ADI ensures today's innovators stay Ahead of What's Possible™. Learn more at www.analog.com and on LinkedIn and Twitter (X) .


Join the Robotic Systems Solutions (RSS) group, where we incubate and mature advanced robotics platforms inside ADI. We explore new system architectures, build rapid prototypes, and develop reference implementations that de-risk future product directions. Our work spans mounted robotics, mobile robotics, and emerging humanoid applications—bridging sensing, motion, connectivity, power, safety, and software to close the gap between concept, feasibility, and scalable platform readiness.

Role Responsibilities:
As part of the RSS platform incubation team, you will:

  • Build and iterate rapid prototypes (electrical, mechanical, firmware, and software) to evaluate new robotics concepts and de-risk system feasibility.

  • Develop end-to-end platform architectures and reference implementations that integrate sensing, compute, connectivity, and motion—creating reusable building blocks for future ADI robotics solutions.

  • Design and execute characterization, benchmarking, and test automation (performance, robustness, safety) to generate data that drives architecture choices and technology readiness.

  • Produce design docs, integration guides, and internal enablement that transfer learnings to product teams—capturing tradeoffs , pitfalls, and recommended system patterns.

Why Join Us?

  • Work on what’s next: Incubate advanced robotics platforms before they become products—shaping future direction through hands-on experimentation.

  • Prototype at pace: Move quickly from idea to proof-of-concept with a team that values fast iteration, measurement, and learning.

  • Build systems intuition: Develop breadth across hardware, firmware, controls, and software—learning how to debug complex, multi-domain robotics systems end-to-end.

  • High leverage influence: Partner with research, product, and applications teams to translate prototypes into reusable platform capabilities and clear technical recommendations.

  • Career development: Get mentorship, training, and exposure across the robotics ecosystem—building foundations for technical leadership in systems engineering, architecture, controls, or embedded development.

What We’re Looking For:
Your role will be tailored to your strengths within the broader team. Relevant skill areas include:

  • Robotics software and controls (ROS/ROS2 a plus; scripting for automation and data analysis a plus)

  • Electronics, lab measurement, and hardware bring-up for sensing, power, and motion subsystems

  • Embedded firmware and real-time development (control loops, drivers, timing, interfaces)

  • System integration, connectivity, and telemetry (industrial interfaces, networking, logging, or edge compute integration)

title: Junior Solution Consultant
description:
The Role

We're looking for a Junior Solution Consultant to join the team at StudioForty9. You'll sit between our clients and our developers — understanding what businesses need, translating that into clear requirements, and helping both sides stay aligned as the work gets built.

This is an entry-level consulting role. We don't expect you to arrive with a fully-formed consulting toolkit. We're looking for someone curious, methodical, and eager to learn the craft of understanding business problems and shaping technical solutions.

This is a fully remote position.

What the Work Actually Looks Like

Your day-to-day will be a mix of investigation, communication, and structured thinking.

You'll spend time with clients — joining calls, asking questions, and working to understand how their business operates and what they're actually trying to achieve (which isn't always what they initially ask for). You'll document requirements, map out processes, and turn messy real-world workflows into something a development team can build against.

On the technical side, you'll be reading through existing code and configurations to understand how things currently work, digging into data to answer questions or spot issues, and testing out new tools and integrations as part of discovery work. You won't be writing production code, but you should be comfortable opening a codebase and figuring out what's going on.

You'll also own your estimates and commitments. If you say something will be done by Thursday, it needs to be done by Thursday — or communicated well before Thursday that it won't be. Reliability matters more than speed.

What We Expect From You

Open-minded and eager to learn, with the ability to independently research, investigate, and apply new technologies and systems while working effectively with limited guidance.

Curiosity and skepticism. We want someone who asks "why?" a lot — not to be difficult, but because understanding the real problem is the core of the job. If a client asks for feature X, we expect you to dig into what they're actually trying to accomplish, not just write down "build X."

Strong ability to understand complex systems and the relationships between technical components and human interactions.

Comfort with ambiguity. The job is rarely clean. Requirements shift, clients don't always know what they need, and you'll often need to form a coherent picture from incomplete information. You should be comfortable saying "I don't know yet, but I'll find out" — and then doing it.

AI-native mindset. We use AI tooling heavily — Claude Code, Cursor, and similar — as part of how we work. You don't need to be an expert, but you should have experimented with agentic AI tools enough to have an opinion about them. If the idea of using AI as a daily work accelerator doesn't interest you, this role won't be a good fit.

Client comfort. You'll be client-facing from early on. You don't need to be a polished presenter, but you should be willing to speak up in meetings, ask clarifying questions, and represent the team professionally.

Requirements

  • A qualification in IT, computer science, or a related technical field — or equivalent self-taught experience you can demonstrate
  • 1–3 years of experience working within an IT team in some capacity (support, QA, development, data analysis, etc.)
  • Comfortable reading and reasoning about code — you don't need to write it fluently, but you should be able to open a codebase and understand the broad strokes
  • Strong written and verbal communication skills — you'll be writing a lot of documentation, tickets, and client correspondence
  • A demonstrated habit of being reliable with deadlines and proactive when things go off track
  • Hands-on experience with AI-assisted tools (Claude Code, Cursor, GitHub Copilot, Codex, or similar)

Nice to Have

  • Exposure to SQL — writing queries, joining tables, making sense of large datasets — or a willingness to learn it
  • Experience with Shopify or other ecommerce platforms, either as a merchant or working on implementations
  • Background in retail operations — understanding of inventory, order management, fulfilment, or POS systems
  • Experience being on client calls or presenting to stakeholders, even informally
  • Familiarity with APIs — being able to read API documentation and test endpoints (Postman, Insomnia, etc.)

Who We Are

StudioForty9 is a recognised leader in the Irish digital sector with a proven track record of delivering and continuously improving high-performance ecommerce solutions for some of Ireland's best-known retail brands.

We work at the intersection of ecommerce strategy, technology, and operations — helping ambitious retailers scale their online businesses, open new channels, and build the integrations and automation that make growth sustainable. We're platform-agnostic, independently minded, and we put long-term client relationships above short-term wins.

Our team is experienced and direct. We hire people who can do the work, take responsibility for it, and make good decisions without needing constant guidance.

To apply, visit: https://studioforty9.com/career/

Submit Your Application

title: QA Automation Engineer
description:
Why Sony Interactive Entertainment?

Sony Interactive Entertainment isn't just the Best Place to Play — it's also the Best Place to Work. Sony Interactive Entertainment (SIE) is the company behind the PlayStation brand. As a subsidiary of Sony Group Corporation, we're part of a proud legacy of innovation and excellence. SIE is a dynamic technology company, delivering cutting-edge hardware and network services to more than 100 million people and an entertainment leader, home to some of the most beloved and recognizable intellectual properties (IP) in the world. Our role at SIE is to create and nurture the experiences under the PlayStation brand, a name synonymous with entertainment excellence and creativity.

Role overview:

SIE Martech Engineering team looking for a highly skilled QA Automation Engineer with 6+ years of experience, who will design, develop, and execute comprehensive test strategies for AEM based web applications, ensuring high quality, scalable, secure, and performant solutions for PlayStation.com and internal applications. The ideal candidate will collaborate closely with architects, developers, product management, and operations teams to deliver robust software solutions and mentor junior QA engineers.

What you'll be doing:

  • Design, create, and execute manual and automated test plans and scripts to validate functionality, performance, and security of AEM sites and web applications.
  • Conduct in-depth functional, regression, integration, performance, and security testing for new or enhanced features.
  • Identify, triage, report, and track defects, ensuring timely resolution and re-testing.
  • Work in an agile, collaborative environment with globally distributed engineering teams.
  • Ensure high-quality, low-defect releases through rigorous testing and process adherence.
  • Develop and maintain automated testing scripts using Java, Selenium WebDriver, Applitools, Cucumber, or other relevant frameworks.
  • Configure CI/CD pipelines using Jenkins, Maven, and related tools to support automated test execution.
  • Mentor and guide junior QA engineers through code reviews, workshops, defect documentation, and knowledge sharing.
  • Collaborate with developers and architects to review requirements, technical designs, and implementation for testability and coverage.
  • Participate in end-to-end QA of web applications, including REST APIs, XML, Xpath validation, and AEM components testing.
  • Evaluate test frameworks and recommend improvements for automation efficiency and coverage.

What we're looking for:

  • Education: BSc/MSc in Computer Science or related field (or equivalent experience).
  • Experience:
  • 6+ years of experience as a QA Engineer, with a focus on web application testing.
  • 4+ years of hands-on experience with Adobe Experience Manager (AEM) and related Adobe products.
  • 4+ years of test automation experience using Java, Selenium WebDriver, Cucumber, or equivalent frameworks.
  • Experience with CI/CD tools (Jenkins, Maven) for automated testing integration.
  • Strong knowledge of web technologies: HTML, CSS, JavaScript, AJAX, XML, REST APIs, and Postman.
  • Expertise in functional, regression, performance, and security testing.
  • Proficient in defect tracking and test management tools such as JIRA, QTest, or Wrike.
  • Experience in reviewing technical designs, AEM components, templates, and services layers to support test planning.
  • Excellent written and verbal communication skills.

Additional skills:

  • Understanding of gaming industry and video game culture is a plus.
  • Experience in mentoring and leading QA teams, including code and test reviews.
  • Organized self-starter with strong ability to prioritize multiple tasks and manage deadlines.
  • Familiarity with performance monitoring, caching mechanisms, and security best practices in web applications.
  • Demonstrable ability to analyze complex business problems and ensure high-quality deliverables.
  • Passionate about continuous improvement and driving QA automation strategy.

Please note, Sony Interactive Entertainment conducts background checks at the offer stage for all new employees (which may include criminal background checks for some roles) and will need to process personal information to support these checks.

Please refer to our Candidate Privacy Notice for more information about what personal information we collect, how we use it, who we share it with, and your data protection rights.

Equal Opportunity Statement:

Sony is an Equal Opportunity Employer. All persons will receive consideration for employment without regard to gender (including gender identity, gender expression and gender reassignment), race (including colour, nationality, ethnic or national origin), religion or belief, marital or civil partnership status, disability, age, sexual orientation, pregnancy, maternity or parental status, trade union membership or membership in any other legally protected category.

We strive to create an inclusive environment, empower employees and embrace diversity. We encourage everyone to respond.

Sony Interactive Entertainment is a Fair Chance employer and qualified applicants with arrest and conviction records will be considered for employment.

title: Application Developer
description:
Key Responsibilities

Technical Leadership & Integration Development

  • Serve as a senior member of the Guidewire integrations team, providing technical guidance throughout the design, development, and implementation phases.

  • Expertly develop integration components using the Gosu programming language.

  • Design and build robust integration solutions leveraging the Guidewire Integration Framework.

  • Provide ongoing support and troubleshooting for technical issues arising during and after the implementation phase.



Solution Design & Quality Assurance

  • Solution Architecture: Design technical solutions that satisfy complex business requirements while maintaining alignment with the overall system architecture.

  • Code Quality: Conduct regular code reviews to maintain high standards and ensure technical excellence across the team.

  • QA Standards: Ensure all developed solutions are thoroughly tested and meet the organization's rigorous quality and performance benchmarks.



Stakeholder Management & Process Improvement

  • Consultancy: Communicate effectively with clients and internal stakeholders to translate business needs into technical feasibility.

  • Project Oversight: Provide consistent updates on project progress and technical milestones to all relevant parties.

  • Continuous Learning: Stay current with the latest advancements in Guidewire technology and proactively recommend improvements to internal processes and engineering practices.



Required Qualifications & Experience

  • Guidewire Expertise: Minimum of 4 years of deep, hands-on experience with the Guidewire InsuranceSuite platform.

  • Integration Development: Minimum of 4 years of proven expertise in Guidewire Integration development using Gosu.

  • Framework Proficiency: Minimum of 4 years of experience designing and developing solutions specifically using the Guidewire Integration Framework.

  • Communication: Exceptional ability to explain complex technical concepts to non-technical stakeholders and clients.

  • Problem Solving: Strong analytical skills with a track record of troubleshooting high-stakes enterprise software issues

title: Junior Telecoms Engineer
description:
Yellowcom are a leading ROI & UK mobile and landline provider, delivering services to businesses from our offices in Dublin, Belfast & Glasgow for over 18 years. We are fully accredited partners with Fixed Line Operators, Mobile Networks and Telecom Manufacturers.

We are looking for a Junior Engineer to join our growing Dublin team. This is an excellent opportunity for someone at the early stages of their career who is eager to develop hands-on experience in telecoms and IT infrastructure.

In this role, you will assist senior engineers with installations at customer sites, including structured cabling and telephone systems (both PBX and hosted). You will also support troubleshooting and maintenance tasks, helping to diagnose and resolve faults both onsite and remotely, while delivering excellent customer service.

This position offers strong development opportunities, with training and exposure to the latest telecoms technologies.

Key Responsibilities

  • Assist with installation of structured cabling and telecom systems
  • Support configuration of phone systems (on-premise and cloud-based)
  • Help diagnose and resolve faults onsite and remotely
  • Work alongside senior engineers on customer projects
  • Maintain clear communication with customers and internal teams
  • Ensure all work is completed to a high standard

The Ideal Candidate Will Have

  • Basic knowledge or interest in structured cabling or telecoms systems
  • Understanding of networking fundamentals (routers, LANs, IP basics)
  • Strong willingness to learn and develop new technical skills
  • Good problem-solving ability and attention to detail
  • Strong communication and interpersonal skills
  • Ability to work both independently and as part of a team
  • A positive attitude and strong work ethic
  • A full Irish Driving License (essential)

What We Offer

  • Excellent training and career development opportunities
  • Hands-on experience with modern telecom technologies
  • Supportive team environment
  • Company vehicle (where applicable)
  • Performance bonus
  • Bike to work scheme
  • Company pension
  • Employee discounts
  • Company events
  • On-site parking

If you’re looking to start your career in telecoms and want to work with a company that invests in your growth, apply today.

Yellowcom is committed to promoting equal opportunities in employment. All applicants will be treated fairly based on merit, skills, and qualifications.

Pay: €30,000.00-€35,000.00 per year

Benefits:

  • Bike to work scheme
  • Company car
  • Company events
  • Company pension
  • Employee assistance program
  • Employee discount
  • Free or subsidised travel
  • On-site parking

Application question(s):

  • Do you hold a full Irish driving licence?
  • Do you have any experience with telecoms, cabling, or IT/networking?
  • What interests you about a career in telecoms engineering?

Work Location: On the road

title: Junior IT Administrator
description:
Job Title: Junior IT Administrator

Location: CJK Finglas Office & Various Site visits

The Junior IT Administrator is responsible for supporting and assisting with all day to day IT systems across CJK, including our main offices and construction project sites. 

The role manages the IT hardware lifecycle, voice/data connectivity, and helps maintain core business and project platforms. It also support for any IT issues, escalating to the Managed Service Provider when required, while supporting Microsoft 365 administration (user lifecycle and access to files, sites, groups and distribution lists) and contributing to cybersecurity controls, training, and awareness.

Duties / Responsibilities:

Infrastructure & Network Management

  • Ensure connectivity for all construction project sites
  • Manage communication lines for sites and staff (all phone and internet lines) 
  • Manage Hardware lifecycle process for IT Assets
  • Maintain AV functionality in office meeting rooms

Business Systems Management

  • Manage IT setup for all new project sites
  • Support project management platforms and core business systems
  • Assist with software licencing monitoring and renewals

Cybersecurity & Compliance

  • Support CJK cybersecurity strategy and controls
  • Ensure correct authorisation and access controls for users
  • Assist with implementation of cybersecurity training, campaigns and projects

MS365 Administration

  • Support User life cycle process (licence management, access to files, SharePoint, groups and distribution lists)
  • Support with development and implementation of SharePoint platform for various departments

IT Support & Service Delivery

  • Manage IT requests for all hardware, software and IT systems. 
  • Provide appropriate escalation of tickets to Managed Service Provider

Data & Reporting

  • Ensure data integrity across systems
  • Assist with AI initiatives

Training and development

  • Co-ordinate all IT training
  • Deliver briefing on CJK IT systems, intranet and cybersecurity to new end users.

Requirements:

  • Proficient in Microsoft Office in Word, Excel, Outlook and PowerPoint
  • At least Intermediate level skills in Excel, experienced with all standard formulae, conditional formatting, tables and charts
  • Minimum 1 year working in IT support/administration role
  • Experienced with SharePoint
  • Lateral thinker with strong organisational, problem-solving and people skills
  • Ability to work on own initiative and as part of a team

Desirable:

  • Experience working in the Construction Sector
  • Experience with Power BI/Data Analytics

company: Society of Saint Vincent de Paul
title: IT Graduate - Dublin (Fixed Term 12months)
description:
IT Graduate - Dublin (Fixed Term 12months)
Location: Dublin 1

Salary range:€30,030 per annum; paid monthly

Experience: Recent graduate (within last 2 years) with a BSc Computer Science or equivalent qualification from an Accredited Institution is essential.

Closing date: Sun, 24 May 2026

Description:
SVP is a large, national, voluntary organisation with extensive experience of working with a diverse range of people who experience poverty and exclusion. Through its network of volunteers and staff, it is strongly committed to working for social justice and advocates the creation of a more just and caring society. SVP employs people to support volunteers in a variety of settings including housing, community care, shops, administration, and other specialist areas.

The Role:

We are currently looking to recruit a IT Graduate (12month Fixed term; 37.5 hours per week) to join ourShared Services - IT team in SVP House, Dublin 1. The purpose of the job is to provide support to IT Microsoft Office platforms and CRM/Agresso Technical support via the IT Helpdesk as well as working with the IT Team on technical issue resolution. The post holder will support roll-out of conference emails to the volunteer userbase of the Society, assist on IT Helpdesk support, and get involved in the transition from in-house data storage server to Cloud based Office 365 SharePoint.

In addition, SVP will afford the post holder the opportunity to participate in a major project whilst getting 'on the job' experience supporting multiple platforms and applications for a user base of 1,000+ users. SVP's strategic platforms are CRM for Membership & Membership related processes, Agresso for finance and Microsoft Office 365. SVP maintain a comprehensive web site that integrates with CRM. Disaster recovery is a key component of IT department deliverables.
This is a great opportunity to join one of Ireland's largest voluntary organisations and make a difference in your community.

Education, Experience, Knowledge and Skills Required

Qualifications
• Recent graduate (within last 2 years) with a BSc Computer Science or equivalent qualification from an Accredited Institution is essential.

Experience
• Previous work experience (paid or voluntary).
• Team working experience.
• Experience working with MS Office tools and/or applications.

Knowledge
• Knowledge of the Society and of its mission and values.
• Knowledge of needs and issues of the poor and disadvantaged.

Skills
• Good communication and interpersonal skills.
• Good problem-solving skills.
• Good logical ability to troubleshoot issues.
• Ability to document events and processes.
• Ability to work well in a team environment.

Full job description for this role and list of associated benefits can be found here: IT Graduate - Job Description 05.2026
I f you meet these requirements and have the desire to join one of Ireland's best known, trusted, and respected not-for-profit organisations please click 'Apply for this job'.
Appointment is subject to satisfactory references. Canvassing will not be accepted and may lead to disqualification. Due to the large volume of applications, we are not in the position to provide individual feedback to candidates who are not shortlisted.

PLEASE NOTE: If you are interested in being considered for thisposition, please apply as soon as possible as the role may be closed in advance of the date shown above due to high volume of applications.

title: IT system administrator - L3 support
description:
IT system administrator - L3 support


Introduction

Our client, a well-established organisation with a strong focus on digital transformation, is seeking an experienced Senior Level 3 IT support engineer to join their growing technology team. This role will suit a highly skilled professional with deep expertise across Microsoft technologies, infrastructure, and advanced support environments.


Company Culture & Values

This organisation promotes a collaborative, forward-thinking environment where continuous improvement and innovation are encouraged. The team values accountability, knowledge sharing, and a proactive approach to problem-solving, with a strong emphasis on service excellence and customer satisfaction.


Salary & Benefits

  • €58,848 - €74,112
  • Opportunity to work with modern Microsoft cloud and hybrid environments
  • Strong focus on professional development and certifications
  • Collaborative and supportive team culture
  • Exposure to large-scale transformation and automation projects


Duties / Responsibilities


Systems Operations

  • Support and manage Microsoft 365 environments including Exchange Online, SharePoint Online, Teams, OneDrive, Intune, and Power Platform
  • Administer hybrid infrastructure including Active Directory, Azure AD (Entra ID), ADFS, Group Policy, and on-premise Exchange/SharePoint
  • Drive automation initiatives using PowerShell, Power Platform, Logic Apps, and AI-based tools
  • Manage on-premise systems and ensure stability of key business applications
  • Own device lifecycle management via Microsoft Endpoint Manager (Intune)
  • Lead system upgrades, implementations, and cloud migration projects
  • Continuously identify opportunities to optimise and automate processes


Advanced Technical Support

  • Provide Level 3 support for critical systems and applications
  • Troubleshoot complex technical issues and deliver root-cause resolutions
  • Act as an escalation point for Service Desk and support teams
  • Manage vendor relationships and escalate issues where required
  • Ensure all incidents are logged and resolved within agreed SLAs
  • Contribute to continuous improvement of support processes and frameworks


Governance & Compliance

  • Monitor system performance, alerts, and maintenance activities
  • Maintain accurate technical documentation and training materials
  • Ensure adherence to ITIL standards and internal governance policies
  • Support audits and penetration testing, implementing recommended improvements


Service Delivery & Excellence


  • Deliver a high-quality, customer-focused support experience
  • Support team development through knowledge sharing and upskilling
  • Promote best practices and ensure adherence to operational standards
  • Lead service improvement initiatives and drive operational efficiency
  • Collaborate with cross-functional teams to support business needs and change initiatives


Key Requirements

  • Minimum 5+ years’ experience in ICT support and administration
  • Strong expertise in Microsoft 365, Entra ID (Azure AD), Active Directory, and hybrid environments
  • Proven experience in automation using PowerShell, Power Platform, or Logic Apps
  • Experience supporting both cloud and on-premise infrastructure
  • Excellent troubleshooting and problem-solving capabilities


Desired Skills

  • Strong communication and stakeholder management skills
  • Experience working within ITIL frameworks
  • Ability to work in fast-paced, evolving environments
  • Strong documentation and organisational skills
  • Proactive mindset with a focus on continuous improvement


Qualifications

  • Relevant third-level qualification (or equivalent experience)
  • Microsoft certifications (M365, Azure, Active Directory, etc.) highly desirable


To be considered for this position apply today or call Vishnu Singh on . Please submit your updated CV in Word Format.
